jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
**ABOUT VOICES FOR CHILDREN**


Founded in 1980, Voices for Children (VFC) transforms the lives of abused, neglected, and abandoned children in foster care in San Diego and Riverside Counties by providing them with trained, volunteer Court Appointed Special Advocates (CASAs). CASA volunteers advocate for a child or sibling group in foster care in court, at school, and in the community to ensure their needs are met. Voices for Children believes every child deserves a safe and permanent home and strives to provide a CASA volunteer to every child in the foster care system who needs one**.** Last year, Voices for Children CASAs and staff provided direct advocacy to more than 2,300 youth in foster care across San Diego and Riverside Counties, as well as case monitoring services to hundreds of other children in foster care who may need an advocate in the future**.**


Voices for Children carries out its work with an annual budget of over $8\.8 million. It is led by a 29\-member Board of Directors and a dedicated staff of 72\. More than 1,200 CASA volunteers form the backbone of the organization, and through their tireless direct work, support children in the foster care system. Voices for Children is part of a statewide and national network of similar organizations.

**OVERVIEW OF THE JOB**


The CEO is an experienced and dynamic senior leader with the vision, expertise, and compassion needed to steward an organization that serves an exceptionally vulnerable population in San Diego and Riverside Counties. This leader is an inspiring and effective communicator with the ability to engage, motivate, and collaborate with an array of internal and external stakeholders including but not limited to Board members, staff, community leaders, individual donors, grant\-makers, CASA allies, and other stakeholders in the child welfare system. Adept at continuous improvement, the CEO is an experienced fundraiser and advocate with a deep understanding of how to lead and manage all aspects of a mission\-driven nonprofit organization. The successful candidate has a passion for the work and a familiarity with the challenges and opportunities inherent in leading an organization that seeks to protect the rights and well\-being of marginalized children.


Among the CEO’s many duties, this leader is responsible for:

**Strategic and Collaborative Leadership**

* Building a positive workplace culture that fosters cohesion and innovation among diverse staff and volunteers throughout San Diego and Riverside Counties
* Engaging program and administrative staff in transparent, supportive conversations that energize staff and produce new strategies for approaching the work
* Working with stakeholders throughout San Diego County, Riverside County, and the State of California to identify and foster collaborative efforts that positively affect the well\-being of abused and neglected children
* Stewarding change within the organization through visionary and strategic work that builds upon the integrity and quality of the existing work

**External Relations and Fundraising**

* Raising the visibility of Voices for Children and increasing awareness of the needs of abused and neglected children through public speaking, promotional activities, and media messaging that inspires engagement
* Collaborating with the Chief Philanthropy Officer to craft and oversee an agency\-wide fundraising plan that includes large\-scale donor events
* Fostering relationships with individuals, corporations, government agencies, and foundations to promote Voices’ work and secure resources to support it
* Advocating at the state and local levels for policies and resources that address the needs of at\-risk children

**Management**

* Operating with integrity, transparency, and accountability in all interactions with internal and external stakeholders
* Overseeing the organization’s financial and operational activities in partnership with senior staff
* Providing ongoing strategic recommendations to the Board based on new programmatic and funding opportunities, trends in the field, staff input and expertise, creativity, financial analysis, and risk assessment
* Working closely with the Board to ensure that programmatic, fiduciary, and legal compliance responsibilities are fulfilled in a transparent and responsible manner

**QUALIFICATIONS**

* A passion for and commitment to addressing the needs of abused and neglected children
* A track record of successfully stewarding a mission\-driven organization
* The ability to monitor and oversee the operations of a nonprofit organization
* Proven executive\-level experience leading teams and communicating, negotiating, and collaborating with diverse internal and external stakeholders
* The ability to speak persuasively to audiences of all types
* Preferred: Graduate degree in a related field such as law, social work, psychology, management, or another relevant field

**Salary range****:** $225,000\-$250,000 with additional bonus incentives and generous benefits

**Applications without a cover letter will NOT be accepted.**

**Application Deadline****:** August 14, 2026 at 5:00 p.m. PDT


Voices for Children is an equal‑opportunity employer committed to inclusive hiring and dedicated to equity, diversity, and culturally responsive service delivery.

**Top Reasons to Work at VFC**


We take pride in our extraordinary employees and their team spirit. Our staff members are dedicated to helping children in foster care and supporting the work of our CASA volunteers.

* We work in a supportive team environment and we set a high bar for ourselves in regard to professionalism and work ethic.
* We truly do make a difference in people’s lives. As an advocacy organization, every day we work to improve the lives and outcomes for abused and neglected children in foster care.
* We are privileged to supervise and support talented and committed CASA volunteers.

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
**Executive Director**

**Financial \& Estate Literacy (It’s Your Money \& Estate)**

**Location:** Orange County, California (Hybrid)

**Position Type:** Full\-Time

**Compensation:** $85,000 – $125,000 annually, commensurate with experience

**Benefits:** Comprehensive benefits package

**About Financial \& Estate Literacy**

Financial \& Estate Literacy (It’s Your Money \& Estate) is a mission\-driven nonprofit organization dedicated to empowering individuals and families with the knowledge and resources needed to make informed financial and estate planning decisions. Through educational programs, community workshops, and strategic partnerships, the organization works to increase financial confidence, promote responsible planning, philanthropic giving and help prevent financial exploitation through awareness and education. Financial \& Estate Literacy is seeking an experienced and visionary Executive Director to lead the organization into its next chapter of growth, impact, and sustainability.

**Position Overview**

The Executive Director serves as the chief executive leader of Financial \& Estate Literacy and is responsible for advancing the organization’s mission through strategic leadership, effective governance partnership, program excellence, community engagement, and operational stewardship. Working closely with the Board of Directors, the Executive Director will translate the organization’s mission and strategic priorities into measurable outcomes while ensuring strong governance practices, financial sustainability, operational effectiveness, and continued program growth. The ideal candidate is a collaborative, mission\-focused leader who can balance strategic vision with hands\-on execution and build meaningful relationships with donors, volunteers, educators, professional advisors, community organizations, and other stakeholders. This is an exciting opportunity for a nonprofit leader who is passionate about education, financial empowerment, and creating lasting community impact.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Executive Leadership \& Strategic Direction** 

* Provide visionary leadership and strategic direction to advance the organization’s mission and long\-term goals.
* Partner with the Board of Directors to develop and implement strategic plans, priorities, and initiatives.
* Translate organizational objectives into actionable programs, measurable outcomes, and sustainable growth strategies.
* Foster a culture of collaboration, accountability, innovation, and continuous improvement.

**Board Governance \& Partnership**

* Serve as the primary liaison between the Board of Directors and organizational operations.
* Support the Board in fulfilling its governance, fiduciary, and strategic responsibilities by providing timely, accurate, and meaningful information.
* Collaborate with the Board Chair and committees on strategic planning, organizational priorities, risk management, and performance evaluation.
* Ensure compliance with nonprofit governance best practices, organizational bylaws, policies, and regulatory requirements.
* Support Board recruitment, development, engagement, and ongoing education.
* Maintain a strong partnership between governance leadership and operational management.

**Program Leadership \& Community Impact** 

* Provide strategic oversight of the organization’s educational initiatives, including It’s Your Money and It’s Your Estate programs.
* Ensure programs are impactful, mission\-aligned, accessible, and responsive to community needs.
* Expand educational outreach through workshops, partnerships, and innovative programming.
* Support facilitators, volunteers, and community partners in delivering high\-quality educational experiences.

**Operations \& Organizational Management** 

* Oversee daily operations and ensure effective systems, processes, and infrastructure.
* Develop and maintain policies, procedures, and organizational practices that support accountability and efficiency.
* Coordinate staff, contractors, volunteers, vendors, and strategic partners.
* Ensure compliance with nonprofit standards and operational requirements.

**Financial Stewardship \& Sustainability**

* Provide leadership for budgeting, financial planning, and responsible resource allocation.
* Work with the Board Treasurer and financial advisors to monitor financial performance and sustainability. Ensure sound financial practices, transparency, and responsible stewardship of organizational resources.
* Identify opportunities to strengthen revenue streams and long\-term financial stability

**Fundraising \& Resource Development**

* Develop and execute fundraising strategies in partnership with the Board and fundraising committees.
* Cultivate relationships with donors, sponsors, foundations, corporations, and community supporters.
* Lead donor engagement, sponsorship development, grant opportunities, and stewardship efforts.
* Communicate organizational impact and funding priorities effectively.

**Community Engagement \& External Relations** 

* Serve as the organization’s primary ambassador and spokesperson.
* Build and maintain partnerships with professional advisors, nonprofits, educational institutions, corporations, and community organizations.
* Represent Financial \& Estate Literacy at events, educational forums, and community meetings.
* Increase awareness of financial literacy, estate planning education, and prevention of financial exploitation.

**Qualifications**

**Required Qualifications**

* Demonstrated executive leadership experience in a nonprofit, educational, community\-based, or mission\-driven organization.
* Experience working with a Board of Directors and understanding of nonprofit governance principles.
* Proven ability to develop strategy, manage operations, and lead organizational initiatives.
* Strong financial management and resource development experience.
* Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
* Ability to inspire

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
**POSITION TITLE:** Director of Development

**WORK SCHEDULE:** Daytime, evenings, and some weekends

**COUNTY:** Kern County

**OFFICE LOCATION:** Kern County \& Fresno County, CA

**SUPERVISORS:** President \& CEO

**SALARY:** $72,800 – 83,200 annually

**TYPE:** Full\-time, Exempt

**Organization Overview:**   
Big Brothers Big Sisters of Central California (BBBSCC) is a nationally recognized, award\-winning agency dedicated to igniting the potential in young people by creating and supporting one\-to\-one mentoring relationships. Serving Madera, Fresno, Tulare, and Kings counties. Our mission is to help children realize their full potential through mentorship, and we are looking for a dynamic Development Director to help sustain and grow our impact.

**Position Summary:**  
The Development Director is a key leadership role responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive fundraising strategy to support and expand BBBSCC’s programs. This role oversees donor relations, grant writing, corporate partnerships, event planning, and overall fundraising efforts. The ideal candidate is a strategic thinker with a passion for youth development and a proven track record of securing funding in the nonprofit sector.

**Key Responsibilities:** 

***Fundraising \& Donor Development***

* Develop and implement an annual fundraising strategy to meet and exceed revenue goals.
* Cultivate and maintain relationships with individual donors, corporate sponsors, and community partners.
* Manage donor stewardship activities, ensuring consistent engagement and recognition.
* Expand the major gifts program through research, cultivation, and solicitation of high\-net\-worth individuals.
* Develop and oversee an effective planned giving program.

***Grants \& Corporate Partnerships***

* Research and identify grant opportunities; write and manage grant proposals and reports.
* Build and maintain relationships with corporate sponsors, securing sponsorships for events and initiatives.
* Oversee compliance and reporting for all secured funding.

***Special Events \& Campaigns***

* Plan, execute, and evaluate major fundraising events, such as Bowl for Kids’ Sake, 300 Club, and other community\-driven initiatives.
* Lead annual giving campaigns, including direct mail and digital fundraising strategies.
* Partner with marketing to develop compelling fundraising materials and donor communications.

***Leadership \& Strategy***

* Work closely with the President \& CEO, Board of Directors, and Development Committee to align fundraising efforts with organizational goals.
* Provide leadership in financial forecasting related to fundraising efforts.
* Maintain donor databases, tracking engagement and impact metrics.

**Qualifications:** 

* Bachelor’s degree in nonprofit management, business, communications, related field OR 5\+ years of experience in nonprofit fundraising, development, or donor relations.
* Proven success in securing major gifts, grants, and corporate sponsorships.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills, with experience in grant writing.
* Excellent relationship\-building skills with donors, sponsors, and community leaders.
* Knowledge of donor management systems (e.g., Aplos \& GiveSmart) is a plus.
* Ability to work flexible hours, including some evenings and weekends for events.

**Compensation:** 

* Compensation salary ranging from $72,800 – 83,200 annually.
* Mileage for use of a personal vehicle for work\-related travel will be reimbursed at the current federal rate.
* Company cell phone will be provided to conduct business calls, text, and email.
* Accumulation of Paid Time Off (PTO) equal to 11 paid holidays and 2 weeks’ paid vacation.
* 100% Vision and Dental and 80% medical health care cover.
* Life insurance.
* Retirement plan with company match.

**How To Apply:**

* Submit a cover letter, resume, and three professional references to info@bigs.org. Applications will be accepted until the position is filled.
* BBBSCC is an equal\-opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

**For more information:**

Big Brothers Big Sisters \| 4047 N Fresno Street \| Fresno, CA 93726 www.bigs.org

* Flexible schedule

* Vision insurance

* Driver's License (Required)

jobs.title: Deputy Director of Institutional Giving
jobs.description:
**Employment Type:**  

Full\-Time/Exempt **Wage:**  

$115,000 \- $125,000 base pay **Department:**  

Philanthropy **Reports To:**  

Director of Institutional Giving **Position Location**  

California – Oakland, Los Angeles, Sacramento, Riverside, Fresno **Work Schedule:**  

Four Day Work Week: Two (2\) days in office, two (2\) days remote. However, there may be certain circumstances, like pressing deadlines or peak business periods, where it becomes necessary for the organization to temporarily switch to a five\-day work week. **WHY THIS ROLE, WHY NOW**


California will define the next decade of climate action, and the resources behind that work have to keep pace. EnviroVoters is scaling its institutional funding program, and we need a senior fundraiser who can lead people, own strategy, and represent the organization with our most important foundation partners. This role sits at the center of a growing team, shaping how we cultivate funders, build the pipeline, and turn ambitious climate and justice campaigns into sustained investment.


This is a leadership role. The Deputy Director of Institutional Giving manages grant staff, drives portfolio strategy across both EnviroVoters (501c4\) and the EnviroVoters Education Fund (501c3\), and serves as a senior relationship holder with institutional funders. If you have led teams, closed six\-figure grants, and want your work to fuel real political power, we want to hear from you.

**What Makes This Role Distinctive**

* Four\-day work week for most of the year with five\-day weeks during peak periods
* Management of a growing institutional giving team, with authority to shape systems, strategy, and portfolio assignments
* Ownership of a portfolio of the organization's most significant institutional funders, with direct responsibility for cultivation, solicitation, and negotiation
* A leadership seat in departmental planning, revenue forecasting, and annual budgeting
* Dual\-entity fundraising across c3 and c4 structures, work that few institutional giving roles offer
* Comprehensive benefits package, including health, vision and dental coverage, 401(k) with employer match,12 days PTO, 12 holidays, a year\-end office closure between Christmas and New Year, and additional benefits that support employee well\-being and work\-life balance

**Position Summary**


As a senior member of the Philanthropy Team, the Deputy Director of Institutional Giving leads the day\-to\-day operation and growth of the institutional giving program for EnviroVoters and the Ed Fund. This role manages grant staff and consultants, owns a portfolio of high\-priority institutional funders, drives annual grant strategy and revenue planning in partnership with the Director of Institutional Giving, and ensures the full grant lifecycle runs with excellence across both entities.

**KEY PRIORITIES**

*Lead and develop the institutional giving team*

* Manage grant staff and consultants supporting the institutional giving portfolio, including hiring, coaching, and performance development
* Set portfolio assignments, workflows, and quality standards across proposals, reports, and stewardship activities
* Evaluate and strengthen systems and processes for grants management, ensuring compliance and alignment with organizational values

*Own strategic funder relationships*

* Manage a portfolio of the organization's largest and most complex institutional funders, leading cultivation, solicitation, negotiation, and stewardship
* Represent EnviroVoters and the Ed Fund with funders and program officers, including independently leading meetings, pitches, and site visits

*Drive portfolio strategy and revenue growth*

* Support in the execution of an annual institutional giving strategy aligned with organizational priorities and revenue goals
* Expand the funder pipeline by identifying, researching, and cultivating new institutional prospects for both entities
* Support the Director of Institutional Giving in annual budgeting, revenue forecasting, and monthly revenue reporting

*Ensure grant management excellence across both entities*

* Oversee production of multi\-year and one\-year proposals and reports, ensuring quality, accuracy, and timely delivery
* Provide internal guidance on permissible grant usage across c3 and c4 entities, including compliance with lobbying and electoral activity restrictions
* Project manage complex, cross\-departmental proposal development and ensure funding agreements are finalized, signed, and paid on schedule
* Work proactively with program, policy, and finance staff to meet all programmatic and fiscal reporting requirements

**What We Need** **From** **You**

* Seven or more years of institutional fundraising experience in a nonprofit environment, including demonstrated success securing and renewing five\- and six\-figure grants
* Two or more years of staff management experience, with a track record of coaching and developing fundraising or grants professionals
* Expertise in cultivating and stewarding external funder relationships, including leading solicitations and negotiations independently
* Working knowledge of 501(c)(3\) and 501(c)(4\) fundraising distinctions, or demonstrated ability to learn and apply them quickly
* Deep understanding of the full grant lifecycle and grant writing craft, with the ability to edit and elevate the work of others
* Ability to manage multiple proposals, deadlines, staff, and stakeholders simultaneously under pressure
* Skilled in persuasive written and oral communication, with the ability to synthesize complex policy work into compelling cases for support
* Exceptional attention to detail, discretion, and ability to maintain confidentiality
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office; experience with Raiser's Edge/RENXT or similar CRM preferred
* Passionate about environmental protection, racial justice, and meaningful social change

**Additional Strengths We Welcome**

* Experience fundraising for advocacy, electoral, o

jobs.description:
**Careers** **by** **E****mpowering Futures, Building Communities**

Imagine going to work each day knowing that your efforts positively impact individuals and communities. As a global movement and the nation's leading nonprofit organization dedicated to strengthening communities through youth development, healthy living, and social responsibility, the YMCA of Greater San Francisco offers more than just a job—we offer a career with a future and the opportunity to make a lasting difference in your community. At the Y, you can uncover your passion and build a lifelong career addressing some of the nation's most pressing social issues.  


**Our****Organizational** **Culture**

At the YMCA of Greater San Francisco, we are committed to demonstrating values that aim to building strong communities where you can **Be**, **Belong** and **Become.** With **Truth \& Courage**, we strive to understand and act on individual and societal truths. We ensure a safe and inclusive environment with **Authenticity \& Accessibility**, allowing everyone to participate according to their needs. Our approach is **Dependable** **\& Creative**, as we respond to community needs through strong partnerships. Above all, we embody **Dignity \& Empathy**, treating everyone with respect and compassion, and recognizing the inherent dignity in all individuals.

**Qualifications**


* 21\+ years old; 3\+ years of experience in youth programs with supervisory responsibilities.
* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in youth development or coaching.
* Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.
* Proficiency with MS Office Suite.
* Availability Monday through Friday, with flexibility to work evenings and weekends as needed. This position is in\-person.
* Experience working with individuals from diverse backgrounds, including but not limited to ethnic, socioeconomic, educational, and religious diversity.
* Familiarity with schools and community\-based organizations.
* Fingerprint and TB test clearance, YMCA\-approved First Aid, CPR, and AED certification.

  

**Preferred Qualifications (Optional)**


* Experience working within SFUSD school system.
* Bilingual in Spanish, Cantonese, or Mandarin.
* Credits for After\-School \& CA State Licensing experience; Y\-USA Team Leader Certification
* Credentialed educator
* Youth Development Trainee
* STEAM program experience
* Salesforce software experience



**Position Summary**

  


The **Youth Program Director I** oversees the planning, development, coordination, and management of out\-of\-school time programs for elementary\-aged youth at the Mission Family YMCA. These programs include after school programming, summer day camp or Power Scholars Academy, and STEAM\-focused initiatives. The Director ensures the delivery of high\-quality, engaging, and inclusive experiences for participants while maintaining compliance with program standards and partner expectations.



The position is responsible for staff supervision, operational oversight, and partnership engagement. This includes supporting and coaching program staff, fostering strong relationships with school administrators, families, community partners, and YMCA colleagues. The Director works collaboratively with senior leadership to align programs with organizational goals and youth development strategies.



Key responsibilities include fiscal oversight (budgeting and contract administration), participating in strategic initiatives within the Youth Development POD, and contributing to the YMCA’s Annual Giving Campaign. The role also serves as a liaison with public and private partners in support of the YMCA’s mission and values.



The position requires adaptability, strong organizational skills, and a commitment to creating a safe, supportive, and enriching environment for all participants and staff.

  

**Program Development \& Management**


* Oversee planning, supervision, and daily operations of Out of School Time programs, including after school care, summer day camp, and out\-of\-school time day camps at an elementary school site.
* Ensure smooth program implementation and alignment with YMCA of San Francisco’s strategic vision, emphasizing safety, quality, and fiscal stewardship.
* Coordinate and support day\-to\-day programming, including educational excursions and activity schedules in partnership with instructors and specialists.
* Design and maintain a safe, inclusive, and welcoming program environment for all participants and staff.
* Develop enrichment and academic activities that meet the Youth Development Principles of the YMCA and partner organizations.
* Incorporate feedback from youth, families, and staff into program planning and curriculum.
* Guide staff in delivering culturally responsive, developmentally appropriate, and engaging activities for children of diverse backgrounds.
* Ensure all programming adheres to child safety protocols and YMCA standards.

  

**Participant Supervision \& Safety**


* Monitor participant well\-being and maintain required staff\-to\-student supervision ratios at all times.
* Identify and respond to incidents and emergencies following YMCA protocols.
* Implement and model consistent safety and behavior guidelines across all programs.
* Ensure all programs comply with safety, risk management, and mandated reporting policies.

* Promote professional growth by modeling YMCA leadership competencies and offering opportunities for development.

**Join a Team Making a Real Difference in the SF Bay Community!**  

  



Do you have a passion for giving back and creating positive change? Do you love working with seniors and want to be part of work that truly makes a difference? If so, the Mercy Brown Bag Program is looking for YOU!
  

Founded over 40 years ago by the senior residents of **Mercy Retirement \& Care Center** in Oakland, we’ve been serving older adults across Alameda County ever since with fresh, healthy groceries. The need has grown as the senior population has boomed, and inflation escalated, so in 2025 we are now serving over 10,000 low\-income older adults with real meaning and purpose.  

  



**Our Executive Director:**  

  



* Establishes and implements the strategic vision for MBBP, including how to scale sustainably by building and refining financial, organizational and operational foundations.
* Drives the overall growth of the program by executing short\- and long\-term goals outlined in the strategic plan.
* Manages strategic growth to increase financial support from donor base and increase food distribution.
* Leads MBBP to ensure timely delivery of quality food products to enrolled clients and oversees all programmatic, operational, financial, advocacy, and fundraising activities of the program.
* Leads the MBBP Advisory Council.
* In partnership with the MBBP Philanthropy Director, researches and solicits funding sources including government grants, corporations, foundations, businesses, service agencies and individuals.
* Solicits and obtains donations for food, supplies, in\-kind services and equipment.
* Develops and maintains close working relationships and provides necessary reporting, documentation and presentations with funding sources and donors.
* Collaborates with team and key stakeholders and team and key stakeholders to formulate and effectively communicate the impact of MBBP, backed by key data and visual tools.
* Continually monitors the external environment for changes that may create opportunities or challenges for MBBP.
* Supports all compliance areas, including funders’ reporting requirements and inventory audits.
* Supports auditors for the annual audit. Develop, monitor, and implement operating budget and collaborates with Finance Team to manage MBBP accounting and financial reporting, ensuring spending aligns with available funding.
* Establishes strategic alliances with organizations.
* Performs Community Outreach through networking with other agencies serving low\-income seniors.
* Maintains program publicity, including press releases, nominations, newspaper articles, magazine articles. Cultivates relationships with associations, corporate organizations, and contributors to raise the profile of MBBP.
* Serves as the primary spokesperson for MBBP.
* Manages, leads, and develops staff to include Food Recovery Program Consultant and Assistant Director, who in turn oversees External and Internal Operations Managers, Development, Volunteer Manager, and all warehouse, and food distribution team members.
* Ensures annual registration at each site. Evaluates on an ongoing basis the effectiveness of existing food distribution sites. Develops new sites as necessary. Ensures information and referrals are provided to clients needing additional services.
Serves as an advocate on issues related to senior hunger and communicating the mission and impact in a manner that inspires participation, reduces stigma, and supports food justice.  
*

jobs.title: Education Program Director
jobs.description:
**Starting Salary: $90,000 \- $105,000 /year based on experience**
 **Environment: Special Education Program, Grades K\-12**
  

**Spectrum Center Schools and Programs**, a growing, dynamic organization with a social mission to offer hope is seeking an **Education Program Director** to join our award\-winning **Special Education** team and perform meaningful work in a culture that welcomes innovation, encourages creative expression, and offers limitless potential for personal and professional satisfaction!



If you thrive in a collaborative, goal\-oriented environment focused on meaningful outcomes for students with special education needs, seek a career filled with genuine purpose, and possess strong problem\-solving skills, compassion and exceptional dedication — ***We Should Talk!***



As the **Education Program Director**, you will play a pivotal role in transforming student outcomes by championing excellence in instruction and fostering an environment of continuous academic growth. Serving as the instructional leader, you will inspire and guide our dedicated staff to consistently deliver high\-quality teaching practices aligned with Spectrum's proven educational strategies. Your leadership will ensure that policies and procedures are implemented effectively, creating a supportive and efficient learning environment where every student can thrive.



This position oversees all site operations and functions as the liaison between the site and the school districts’ designated site contacts by establishing and maintaining positive, interactive, working relationships.


**‖ Responsibilities Include:**


* Providing visionary leadership to drive student achievement and ensure smooth, effective operations by supervising staff, developing and implementing performance management plans, identifying professional learning needs, and engaging in continuous professional growth opportunities.

* Analyzing staff professional development needs and aligning them with Spectrum’s instructional vision while recognizing achievements, addressing concerns, and maintaining productive, solutions\-focused site operations.
* Leading the induction and training of new staff—either directly or through designees—to ensure a positive onboarding experience and maximize the success and retention of new team members.
* Monitoring facilities and programs to ensure the safety of students and staff at all times, including compliance with contractual and state requirements, adherence to staffing ratios, and readiness to implement crisis protocols.
* Performing essential managerial functions such as overseeing daily school operations, managing schedules and activities, coordinating custodial and maintenance efforts, supervising food and transportation services, directing administrative and budgetary processes, allocating resources, and fostering positive district and community relationships.
* Demonstrating fiscal responsibility by strategically maximizing resources in partnership with the direct supervisor to support instructional priorities and student outcomes.
* Participating in the enrollment process from initial referral to final day of attendance, ensuring timely placement, conducting effective site tours, managing rosters per student needs and state mandates, and contributing to transition planning toward the least restrictive environment when applicable.
* Instructing, monitoring, and evaluating staff and students in the effective use of learning materials and resources to ensure instructional fidelity and optimal student performance.

* Compiling, analyzing, and utilizing assessment data to track student growth, inform instruction, and guide targeted academic interventions.
* Maintaining accurate, complete, and timely records of student progress in compliance with state laws, Spectrum guidelines, and district policies.

* Ensuring compliance with all mandatory requirements, including the IEP process, state assessments, and Spectrum policies.
* Promoting a positive school culture by fostering programs and practices that encourage appropriate student behavior, applying support or disciplinary measures as needed in alignment with Spectrum policies.
* Providing guidance, encouragement, and academic counseling to students facing challenges to help them achieve their goals.
* Performing other duties as assigned to support the overall success of the program and its students.

jobs.title: Executive Assistant to the CEO
jobs.description:
**About Canvas Institute**

Canvas Institute is a Staten Island\-based nonprofit that has served over 60,000 youth and residents through school programming and community engagement. Our work spans arts and culture, youth development, civic engagement, and health and wellness, including our Compassionate Systems social\-emotional learning programming delivered across more than 30 New York City Public Schools. We are a licensed NYC Public Schools vendor, a Project Pivot Partner, and an NYC Civic Corps (AmeriCorps) host site.

**Position Summary**

The CEO is looking for a highly organized, technology\-savvy, and proactive professional to serve as Executive Assistant to the CEO while also keeping the office and day\-to\-day operations running smoothly and providing administrative support across the organization’s projects and grants. This is a hands\-on, multi\-faceted role that blends three areas of work: directly supporting the CEO, managing the office and its administrative systems, and serving as the administrative backbone for the programs, projects, and funded initiatives Canvas Institute takes on.

The right person can anticipate needs, stay several steps ahead, juggle competing priorities, and handle sensitive organizational matters with discretion and follow\-through. Canvas Institute is building an AI\-first team, and the ideal candidate is fluent with digital tools and knows how to leverage AI to work faster and smarter. We are looking for a problem\-solver who takes ownership and gets things done without being asked twice.

**Key ResponsibilitiesExecutive Support to the CEO**

**Schedule and calendar management**

* Build and maintain the CEO’s calendar in Google Calendar, including meetings, program visits, community events, and travel
* Protect the CEO’s time by prioritizing requests and resolving scheduling conflicts
* Prepare the CEO for each day with agendas, materials, and reminders

**Direct support and correspondence**

* Track the CEO’s tasks, deadlines, and follow\-ups, and keep everything organized in one place
* Manage correspondence, including drafting, sending, and routing important documents and letters
* Handle time\-sensitive matters such as mailing official correspondence
* Coordinate appointments and administrative needs on the CEO’s behalf
* Serve as a dependable point of contact who keeps the CEO’s professional logistics running smoothly

**Office Management**

* Keep the office running day to day, ensuring it is organized, stocked, and ready for staff, students, and visitors
* Order and manage office supplies, program materials, and equipment, and track what has been purchased
* Serve as the point of contact for vendors, deliveries, building needs, and basic facilities coordination
* Maintain organized files and records across Google Workspace, both digital and physical, and keep shared drives and folders structured and up to date
* Establish and maintain simple office systems and routines that help the whole team stay organized
* Provide general administrative support to staff and help keep internal operations moving

**Errands and on\-the\-ground support**

* Run organizational errands on the CEO’s and the office’s behalf, including trips to the post office, bank, and other locations
* Pick up and deliver documents, packages, and materials as needed
* Provide reliable transportation for errands and time\-sensitive tasks

**Project and Grant Administration**

Canvas Institute regularly takes on funded programs, partnerships, and grant\-supported projects that require careful paperwork, tracking, and reporting. This role serves as the administrative lead that keeps those projects compliant, documented, and on track behind the scenes.

* Serve as the administrative point person for the organization’s programs, projects, and grants, handling the paperwork and documentation required on behalf of Canvas Institute
* Complete, submit, and file the forms and paperwork tied to funded programs and partnerships, and make sure deadlines are met
* Track participant information for programs such as youth employment and workforce initiatives, including enrollment paperwork, attendance, time and hours, and required documentation
* Support partnership and civic engagement projects by completing required paperwork, coordinating logistics, and documenting trainings and activities
* Maintain complete, well\-organized records for each project, ensuring everything is also captured and backed up digitally in the right folders
* Assist with data collection, reporting, and documenting outcomes and processes for projects, grants, and funded programs
* Keep project files audit\-ready so that documentation can be produced quickly when funders or partners request it

**Required Qualifications**

* Valid driver’s license and access to a reliable personal vehicle (required, both are essential to this role)
* Strong technology skills and hands\-on experience with Google Calendar and Google Workspace (Gmail, Docs, Drive, Sheets)
* Comfort and familiarity with AI tools, with the ability to leverage AI to complete tasks efficiently (Canvas uses Claude and ChatGPT internally)
* Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities, projects, and deadlines at once
* Attention to detail and accuracy, especially with paperwork, recordkeeping, and documentation

* High level of discretion, professionalism, and trustworthiness with confidential matters
* Proactive, resourceful, and able to work independently with minimal direction
* Availability for a flexible schedule, including occasional evenings or weekends tied to events

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Prior experience as an executive assistant, office manager, administrative coordinator, or in a comparable support role
* Experience supporting grant\-funded or program\-based work, including paperwork, compliance, recordkeeping, or reporting

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
**Job Summary**  

  

Reporting directly to the Board of Trustees, the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) oversees the overall direction of the agency, while ensuring alignment with agency mission, objectives and goals. Experienced in managing the day\-to\-day operations of an organization, this individual must possess the qualities and skills to maintain a great company culture, provide effective and inspiring leadership, establish great working relationships with the Board of Trustees, employees, grantors, the community and stakeholders, and be the driving force in the agency’s development towards long\-term success. The CEO of the JRF is chiefly responsible for the following key areas through the essential duties and responsibilities outlined below.  

  

**Responsibilities:*** Ensures compliance with all regulatory agencies governing a FQHC and a CAA. Continually monitors operations, programs, physical properties. Initiates appropriate changes.
* Through strategic planning meetings, establishes current and long\-term goals, objectives, plans and policies, subject to approval by the Board of Trustees; the CEO ensures organizational compliance.
* With the approval of the Board of Trustees, the CEO establishes current and long\-term goals, objectives, plans and policies through strategic planning meetings with elected committee. The CEO is ultimately responsible for ensuring organizational compliance that is consistent with our Strategic Plan.
* Assures the organization and its mission, programs, and services are consistently presented as a strong positive image to the community.
* Dispenses advice, guidance, direction, and authorization to carry out major plans, standards and procedures, consistent with established policies and board approval.
* On a scheduled basis meets with the Executive team to ensure operations are being executed under the organization's policies. Oversees the adequacy and soundness of the organization's financial structure, and remains conversant with financial management practices in order to support the Chief Finance Officer (CFO) in decision\-making to assure financial health and stability.
* Reviews operating results of the organization, compares them to established objectives, and takes steps to ensure that appropriate measures are taken to correct unsatisfactory results.
* Plans and directs all investigations and negotiations pertaining to mergers, joint ventures, the acquisition of businesses, or the sale of major assets with approval of the Board of Trustees.
* Establishes and maintains an effective system of communications throughout the organization.
* Takes part in, and coordinates the selection and training of new board members, keeps existing board members informed about current trends, problems and medical activities to facilitate policy\-making, and serves as liaison between Board and JRF staff.
* Oversees fundraising planning and implementation, including identifying resource requirements, researching funding sources, establishing strategies to approach funders, submitting proposals and administrating fundraising records and documentation.
* Plans, coordinates, and controls the daily operation of the JRF through the agency’s Directors and program leads.
* Directly supervises Program Directors and carries out supervisory responsibilities in accordance with the organization’s policies and applicable laws.
* Active involvement in the recruiting and retention of effective leaders in senior management positions. Responsibilities include interviewing, hiring, and training employees; planning, assigning, and directing work; appraising performance; rewarding and disciplining employees; addressing complaints and resolving problems.
* Responsible for overseeing all claims management activities; including processing of claims related activities and serves as point of contact.
* Serves on QA/QI/RM Committee and is involved in all QA/QI/ Risk Management activities.
* Responsible for strong public relations and marketing programs.
* Inclusive of other duties not outlined within the scope of the position, geared towards the overall success of the agency.

**Desired Attributes**  

The Chief Executive Officer position requires a visionary, mission\-oriented, and entrepreneurial individual who thrives in a relatively fast\-paced environment. Ideal candidates for this position will bring a variety of experiences and attributes to the position, including:* Master’s degree (M.A.) or Bachelor’s with five to ten years’ related experience and/or training; or combination of education and experience.
* Management experience in non\-profit, healthcare, and/or local government; FQHC and health related experience preferred.
* Experience and skill in working with a Board of Trustees.
* Strong understanding of integral business and administrative functions, inclusive of finance, human resources, compliance and grant management.
* Effective leadership and organizational skills in relation to strategic planning, delegating, and business development.
* Innovative with strong financial management skills and experience in generating new revenue streams and managing complex budgets, inclusive of budget preparation, analysis, decision\-making and reporting.
* Astute, with strong decision\-making and problem solving skills.
* Working knowledge of data analysis, performance metrics, and business infrastructure.
* Experience in public relations, marketing, and fundraising.
* Strong networker with excellent communication skills, both internally and externally, with the ability to positively represent the organization and its mission across a wide range of stakeholders and constituencies.
* Strong community awareness with an interest in being at the forefront of community engagement.
* Demonstrates commitment to the values of diversity, inclusiveness and empowerment.
* Proficiency and high comfort level with Microsoft Office Suite preferred (MS Word, Excel, Power Point, and Outlook)
* Person

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
**Dominican Academy**  
Since 1897, Dominican Academy has been educating young women in the Catholic tradition of Saint Dominic, offering a rigorous academic program distinguished by a firm commitment to values of truth, integrity, prayer, community, and service. As a Catholic, all\-honors high school sponsored by Dominican Veritas Ministries and located on the Upper East Side of Manhattan, Dominican Academy challenges and empowers young women to become spiritual, intellectual, moral, and socially responsible leaders in a global society. Dominican Academy is proud of its current association with Dominican Veritas Ministries, a broad coalition of Dominican\-sponsored ministries.

**Position Overview:**  
Reporting directly to the President, the Director of Development leads the planning, structure, and implementation of the school's expansive development program. Well versed in D.A.'s mission and the lived experience of the Four Pillars, the Director provides strategic direction, carries a personal portfolio of major donors, and serves as the school's spokesperson in its philanthropic efforts.

The Director works in close partnership with the President on major\-donor strategy, collaborates with the Communications team on the annual report, alumnae magazine, and appeals, and builds the long\-range advancement plan hand in hand with the Vice President for Finance.

The team you will lead:

**Development Database \& Annual Fund Manager**: Owns gift processing, database integrity, and the annual fund engine that keeps the program running.

**Alumnae Relations \& Special Events Manager**: Leads alumnae engagement and the school's signature events, including the Spring Auction \& Benefit.

**Grant Writing Consultant**: Supports foundation research, proposal writing, and grant follow\-up under the Director's direction.

**Primary Responsibilities:**

**Strategic Vision \& Planning**

● Partner with the President to set the vision, goals, and priorities of D.A.’s mission advancement efforts.  
● Build a long\-range plan and annual goals for the Development Office, in close coordination with the VP for Finance.  
● Establish KPIs and metrics to track progress against school\-wide fundraising goals.  
● Prepare and manage the Development Office budget and report progress to the President and Board.  
● Serve as a trusted liaison to the Development Committee Chair and Board of Trustees.

**Team Leadership \& Operations**

● Lead all aspects of the development plan: major gifts, annual appeal, grant writing, alumnae engagement, and events.  
● Supervise, evaluate, and develop Development Office team members.  
● Ensure accurate gift recording, timely acknowledgment, and effective use of the donor database.  
● Oversee foundation giving, including grant writing, budgets, funder contact, and follow up.

**Community \& Engagement**

● Direct programming for alumnae engagement and the school’s special events.  
● Collaborate with Communications on the annual report, alumnae magazine, and appeals.  
● Be a present, engaged presence at school\-wide events as an ambassador for the mission.  
● Work collaboratively across offices and with faculty to support school initiatives.

Successful candidates will work collegially with colleagues, serve as ambassadors of all\-girls Catholic education in the Dominican tradition, and possess the listening and storytelling skills to understand and communicate donors’ values and commitment to DA. Professionalism, thoughtful attention to confidential information, effective communication skills, and prioritization of complex, demanding work are essential qualities for the Director of Development.

**The Ideal Candidate: A Forward\-Facing Leader with Real Hustle**

● A closer, not just a cultivator. A forward\-facing presence with genuine hustle, who converts relationships and warm leads into gifts and follows through to the ask.  
● Visionary and strategic. Sees the whole board, sets an ambitious plan, and has the discipline and metrics to reach it.  
● Financially fluent. A strong sense of budget and reporting, and a natural, credible partner to the Vice President for Finance.  
● An authentic ambassador. Believes in Catholic, Dominican education and can speak to the Four Pillars with warmth and conviction.  
● A storyteller and listener. Understands donors’ values and tells D.A.’s story in a way that moves people to give.

**Qualifications:**

● **5 to 10 years** in nonprofit development with increasing fundraising and supervisory responsibility.  
● **Demonstrated success** in major\-gift cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship.  
● **Proven ability** in grant and proposal writing and securing commitments from individuals and foundations.  
● **A belief** in Catholic Dominican education and the pillars of prayer, study, community, and service.  
● **Ability to cultivate donors** locally and nationally, with a high degree of professionalism.  
● **Strong CRM skills** with Raiser’s Edge experience preferred; CFRE certification preferred.

*A* ***Bachelor’s degree*** *required, along with an uncompromising sense of discretion and confidentiality. Some evening and weekend work is required.*

**Application Process:**

Applicants should email resume to Marie Keith, Vice President of Education/Catholic Recruiter Associates at marie@CatholicRecruiter.com

**Application Deadline:**

August 15, 2026 \-\- Note: we reserve the right to shorten the deadline for applications if we have sufficient interest. Please apply early to ensure your background will be considered.

Only those selected for an interview with the search committee will be contacted.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: From $110,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

Capital Preparatory Schools is a charter school management organization operating a network of educational communities. Our mission is to build a path where there was none. The Capital Preparatory Schools has four school locations: one in Harlem, one in the Bronx, New York, and two locations in Bridgeport, Connecticut providing students in grades K–12 with a year\-round, college\-preparatory education that encourages students to become scholars and agents of change.

Serving concurrently as Executive Director of the Hill\-Murray Foundation, the Vice President partners closely with the Foundation Board of Governors to provide strategic advancement leadership, governance support, organizational management, and fundraising counsel. The successful candidate is a visionary leader, accomplished fundraiser, exceptional relationship builder, and trusted advisor with a demonstrated ability to cultivate transformational philanthropic partnerships, inspire generosity, develop high\-performing teams, and build an advancement program comparable to those of the nation's leading independent schools and small colleges. All Hill\-Murray employees are expected to support, model, and champion HM’s mission statement: *Hill\-Murray empowers students to reach their fullest potential by inspiring them to utilize their God\-given strengths to become leaders who will make a positive difference in the world.*

jobs.title: Director of Advancement - 2025813
jobs.description:
Are you a person of compassion? An advocate for justice? Someone who stands up for the rights of the vulnerable and speaks out for the marginalized, the exploited and the forgotten? Do you believe in our calling as Christians to welcome the least of these and love our neighbor?  

If you answered ‘yes’, to any of the above, World Relief, and millions of people around the world need you.  

  

At this pivotal moment in time, we are rapidly expanding and growing our team to meet the increasing needs of our world. We are looking for people who want to use their gifts and talents to make a real and tangible difference in our world and the lives of the suffering.

If you’re looking for a purpose\-driven career in which you can grow your talents, while also standing up for the rights of the vulnerable, we want you to join us today.  

**ORGANIZATION SUMMARY**
World Relief is a global Christian humanitarian organization whose mission is to boldly engage the world’s greatest crises in partnership with the church. The organization was founded in the aftermath of World War II to respond to the urgent humanitarian needs of war\-torn Europe. Since then, for 80 years, across 100 countries, World Relief has partnered with local churches and communities to build a world where families thrive and communities flourish.  

  

Today, organizational programming focuses on humanitarian and disaster response, community strengthening and resilience, and refugee \& immigrant services and advocacy. **POSITION SUMMARY:**

World Relief seeks a Director of Advancement for the Chicagoland Region that will lead local office fundraising, communications and external engagement strategies. This role provides strategic leadership for all advancement functions in Chicagoland, ensuring alignment with broader World Relief priorities and organizational goals. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in fundraising, a history of compelling communications, a collaborative leadership approach, and a strong results orientation.
### **ROLE \& RESPONSIBILITIES:**

* This role is responsible for supervising others and includes responsibilities consistent with supervising employees, including but not limited to monitoring daily work activities, tracking and approving timesheets including approving time off, monitoring performance including entering goals, progress check\-ins, and weekly or bi\-weekly check\-ins, documenting performance concerns, entering staff promotions, transitions, and separations in HRIS within required time frame. Managers at World Relief are also considered spiritual leaders and are expected to lead staff in a manner consistent with our Christian values, including but not limited to praying with staff, leading devotionals, and fostering a Christian environment in interactions with staff throughout World Relief.

Strategic Leadership
* Provide leadership and strategic guidance related to World Relief's mission, fundraising strategy, and external engagement efforts in Chicagoland
* Develop and implement annual private fundraising plans, including goals, key performance metrics, timelines, and accountability measures, in collaboration with Executive Director and US Advancement Team

Team Leadership and Administration

* Maintain an environment where all team members can bring their ideas to the table, ensuring the best solutions to challenges

Strategic Donor Engagement and Growth
* Lead strategies to identify, cultivate, and acquire new major and mid\-level donor prospects
* Grow and manage a portfolio of 50 major donors through personalized engagement, consistent communication, and meaningful recognition of their impact
* Engage in donor meetings and speak at churches/ with church partners on a regular basis
* Partner with positions that report nationally, including Portfolio Development Manager and Strategic Partnership Director to ensure coordinated strategy and relationship management
* Collaborate with program teams and communications staff to connect donors with mission outcomes, and create opportunities for shared learning and deeper partnership in advancing organizational goals

Communications

* Ensure branding, messaging, tone, and voice are consistent with World Relief’s values and posture
* As needed, write and/or edit fundraising and communications content, including appeals, campaigns, and donor communications
* Lead alignment across external channels of World Relief Chicagoland including Church Mobilization and Volunteer Mobilization
* Represent World Relief Chicagoland in media opportunities and ensure staff are well\-prepared and effectively positioned as subject matter experts when

jobs.title: Development Director
jobs.description:
**Organization Overview**

The original Freedom Quilting Bee (Bee), was a women’s quilting cooperative established in 1966 during the tumultuous times of the Civil Rights Movement. The cooperative manufactured quilts for the largest retailers in America; Sears, Bloomingdale’s and interior designers in New York City.

***Our Mission \- To teach, share and preserve threads of quilting arts, resistance \& legacy of Black women in Gee’s Bend and Alberta, Alabama.***

***Our Vision \- To inspire makers across the globe, sharing threads of transformative art, culture, and the pioneering legacy of Black women in Gee’s Bend and Alberta, Alabama.*** 

Freedom Quilting Bee Legacy (Legacy) was founded to ensure the heroic, historic, innovative, artistic and incredible odds\-beating work of the women of the original Bee isn’t lost to history. We celebrate and teach about the work and accomplishments of those women (and men) past and present who made the Bee such a powerful artistic and community force. We will offer real economic opportunities for a new generation of quilt makers and artisans.

We provide solutions to community needs, such as Internet access, adult learning and skills building.

We draw tourism to the area and offer a deeper experience of the history and current activities, with shops, lodging, classes and interactive exhibits.

We preserve the past and evolve into the future by creating jobs, building wealth and teaching life skills to improve our community and its people..

Our nonprofit organization is dedicated to making a positive impact on our community by providing outreach, access to history and tourism. We rely heavily on fundraising to sustain our programs and services, and we are seeking a Director of Development to lead our fundraising efforts.

**Job Summary**

The Director of Development is responsible for leading and executing the organization's revenue development strategy, with a focus on individual donations, corporate donations and sponsorships, foundation grants, and governmental grants. This role is ideal for a strong relationship builder, strategic communicator, and results\-oriented professional whose prior experience may come from business development, sales, account management, marketing, public relations, proposal writing, community engagement, or another transferable field.

While prior nonprofit or fundraising experience is helpful, it is not required. The successful candidate will bring a skillset that lends itself well to donor cultivation, partnership development, persuasive communication, opportunity identification, proposal development, and revenue goal achievement. This position will work closely with leadership, board members, staff, external partners, and funders to build sustainable financial support for the organization's mission and programs.

**Primary ResponsibilitiesFundraising Strategy and Revenue Development**

* Develop and implement an annual development plan that supports the organization's financial goals and long\-term sustainability.
* Identify, evaluate, and pursue opportunities for individual giving, corporate support, foundation funding, and government grants.
* Build and maintain a pipeline of prospects, active donors, funders, sponsors, and strategic partners.
* Track fundraising activity, revenue progress, deadlines, and follow\-up needs to ensure accountability and timely execution.

**Individual Donations**

* Cultivate and steward relationships with individual donors, including prospective donors, recurring donors, and major gift prospects.
* Create donor engagement strategies that communicate the organization's mission, impact, and funding needs in a clear and compelling way.
* Support donor outreach, appeals, acknowledgments, recognition efforts, and ongoing relationship management.

**Corporate Donations and Sponsorships**

* Develop relationships with businesses, corporate partners, and community stakeholders to secure donations, sponsorships, and partnership support.
* Create sponsorship materials, proposals, and partnership opportunities aligned with the organization's programs and visibility needs.
* Maintain strong communication with corporate partners to support retention, renewals, and expanded engagement.

**Foundation and Governmental Grants**

* Research foundation and governmental grant opportunities that align with the organization's mission, programs, and funding priorities.
* Coordinate the preparation and submission of grant proposals, applications, budgets, narratives, and supporting documentation.
* Work with leadership and program staff to gather accurate program information, outcomes, and financial details for proposals and reports.
* Monitor grant deadlines, reporting requirements, renewal opportunities, and compliance\-related follow\-up.

**Communications, Reporting, and Relationship Management**

* Prepare persuasive written materials, including donor communications, grant narratives, sponsorship proposals, impact summaries, presentations, and reports.
* Use data, outcomes, and stories to communicate the value and impact of the organization's work.
* Maintain accurate donor, funder, and partner records in the organization's CRM, donor database, or tracking system.
* Collaborate with the Executive Director, Board of Directors, staff, and volunteers to support fundraising efforts and external relationship building.
* Represent the organization professionally at meetings, community events, funder conversations, and partnership opportunities.

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor's degree in communications, business administration, public administration, marketing, nonprofit management, or a related field.
* Demonstrated success building and managing relationships with clients, customers, donors, community partners, funders, or other stakeholders.
* Experience in business development, account management, sales, marketing, public relations, grant writing, customer success, community engagement, partnership dev

jobs.title: Development Director
jobs.description:
*Student U is an education nonprofit rooted in Durham.* ***We are students, families, and educators cultivating joy and academic excellence in learning communities so all students thrive in Durham and beyond.***  


*We believe everyone deserves opportunities in their educational journey that enable them to reach their full potential. This is not yet the reality for every student. Structural racism, intergenerational poverty, and other injustices create significant barriers for first\-generation college\-bound students and entire communities across Durham and the U.S., including here in Durham, N.C.*  


*We support students and their families inside and outside of school, helping them overcome challenges to reach college graduation. At the same time, we work to improve the public education system by sharing successful strategies with schools and educators and building a strong community at The W.G. Pearson Center, aiming for a better future for our youth.**We show up for students and families with joy, rigor, and deep belief that every person is worthy of opportunities that help them grow and thrive.*
*That same culture extends to our team. We are a collaborative, growth\-minded staff that invests in each other the same way we invest in students. We celebrate wins, learn from challenges, and are committed to building an organization where everyone can do the best work of their career. If you are looking for a place where your skills will matter, your voice will be heard, and your work will directly change lives in Durham and across North Carolina, you have found it.*


**POSITION OVERVIEW**



Student U has grown from a $93,000 budget in 2007 to an estimated $6\.1M budget in 2026, and we are ready for the next chapter. We are building a robust individual major gifts program while continuing to deepen and diversify our relationships with foundations, government funders, and corporate partners. This is a rare opportunity to join a high\-performing advancement team at an inflection point and help shape what comes next.



The Development Director will serve as a key leader on the advancement team, reporting directly to the Chief Advancement Officer and working in close partnership with the Executive Director. This is a relationship\-centered, externally\-facing role for someone who is energized by connecting people to meaningful work and has the experience to translate those relationships into long\-term investment in our mission. The Development Director will own and lead Student U’s Major Gifts program, carrying a personal portfolio of donors at the 5\-figure gift level while also serving as a key partner to the Executive Director in managing relationships with our top 20 donors.

  


**JOB RESPONSIBILITIES**


**Individual Giving, Major Gifts, and Donor Stewardship (70%)**


* Build, lead, and continuously refine Student U’s Major Gifts program, serving as its primary architect and day\-to\-day driver
* Carry and grow a personal portfolio of major donors and prospects at the 5\-figure gift level, developing individualized engagement strategies to secure one\-time and multi\-year commitments
* Serve as a strategic partner to the Executive Director in managing relationships with Student U’s top 20 donors, providing preparation, follow\-through, and relationship continuity to support the Executive Director’s caseload
* Lead outreach, cultivation, and stewardship across your portfolio with warmth, intentionality, and consistent follow\-through
* Design and implement a meaningful stewardship program that keeps current donors connected, informed, and inspired by their impact on Student U students and families
* Ensure donors at every level feel genuinely valued through timely acknowledgment, personalized touchpoints, and regular impact updates
* Develop and implement a tiered recurring giving program to grow sustainable individual revenue
* Work closely with the Board of Directors to deepen board giving, strengthen board member engagement in fundraising, and build a culture of philanthropy at the governance level

  


**Foundations, Government, and Corporate Relationships (15%)**
* Support the growth of a portfolio of foundation, government, and corporate relationships, serving as a strategic thought partner
* Collaborate with the Director of Partnerships and the Grants Committee to ensure proposals are timely, accurate, compelling, and aligned with Student U's work and funder priorities
* Represent Student U at site visits, community events, and funder convenings

  


**Development Operations and Collaboration (15%)**
* Partner with the Data Manager to ensure the donor database (Salesforce) is maintained with accuracy and integrity, and that donor acknowledgment is prompt and meaningful
* Coordinate with the Chief Advancement Officer and Director of Finance on revenue forecasting and cash flow projections
* Bring a continuous improvement mindset to development systems, strategies, and processes, identifying opportunities to strengthen infrastructure and outcomes
* Serve as an engaged collaborator across teams, particularly with the program team, to maintain a clear and compelling picture of Student U's impact for donors

 **REQUIREMENTS \& KEY COMPETENCIES**



You are a seasoned development professional who leads with relationships and follows through with results. You know how to build authentic connections with donors and move them from curiosity to commitment, and you understand that keeping a donor is just as important as finding one. You are equally comfortable in a one\-on\-one donor conversation, on a stage presenting to a room, and in a spreadsheet tracking your pipeline.



You believe, as we do, that fundraising is an act of community\-building. You do not see donors as transactions. You see them as long\-term partners in a shared vision for Durham, and you take seriously the responsibility to keep them engaged and connected to the impact of their giving.

Overview

The Director of Development (DD) leads the strategy, execution, and performance of all fundraising efforts that support the overall mission and growth of the Boys \& Girls Clubs of Muncie (BGCM). The DD is also responsible for overseeing marketing and communications to ensure alignment with organizational goals and to strengthen visibility, engagement, and donor support.

Description

Mission: To inspire and enable all young people, especially those who need us most to reach their full potential as productive, caring, and responsible citizens.

Job Description

Job Title: Director of Development

FLSA Classification: Exempt

Salary Range: $48,000 \- $58,000

Reports to: CEO

Supervisory responsibilities: Development Staff

Summary/objective The Director of Development (DD) leads the strategy, execution, and performance of all fundraising efforts that support the overall mission and growth of the Boys \& Girls Clubs of Muncie (BGCM). The DD is also responsible for overseeing marketing and communications to ensure alignment with organizational goals and to strengthen visibility, engagement, and donor support.

Essential duties and responsibilities include the following and may be completed with or through subordinate personnel. Other duties may be assigned. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform these essential functions.

Every staff member is expected to cultivate and maintain positive donor relationships.

· Create, lead, implement, and execute a comprehensive development strategy, including oversight of marketing and public relations efforts.

· Identify, create, and maintain opportunities to increase public awareness of Club services, programs, and impact.

· Lead all corporate and individual giving efforts, including expanding strategies such as major gifts, planned giving (estate planning, bequests), and recurring giving programs.

· Partner with the CEO and Board of Directors to develop and execute donor engagement strategies, including face\-to\-face visits, stewardship plans, and annual campaign initiatives. Maintain and grow relationships with donors, prospects, foundations, and other funding sources.

· Serve as an ambassador and spokesperson for the organization, clearly communicating the mission and impact of BGCM to internal and external audiences.

· Lead grant development efforts, including prospect research, proposal writing, grants calendar management, and reporting progress to the CEO.

· Provide leadership and vision for fundraising events and campaigns that drive revenue and community engagement.

· Participate in meetings as appropriate and communicate effectively in both written and verbal formats. May present to the Board of Directors, stakeholders, or community partners as needed.

· Engage in professional development opportunities and networking events as approved by the CEO.

Work environment

· Able to stand and/or walk up to 25% of time. Able to sit up to 50% of the time. Able to view a computer monitor for 6\-8 hours per day. Some travel may be required in company or personal vehicle. Must be able to lift up to 25 pounds at times. The role may be eligible for some hybridized/WFH opportunity at the discretion of the CEO, and based upon the availability of technology.

· Employee required to possess a valid driver’s license and is occasionally required to transport members in a company vehicle.

· Proof of vehicle insurance is required.

jobs.title: Chief Development Officer
jobs.description:
**POSITION OVERVIEW**

The Chief Development Officer is an in\-person role responsible for the successful growth of Nashville PBS. The CDO will create, drive, and implement a comprehensive revenue strategy and plan that supports both immediate and long\-term growth. Strong leadership skills are a must as this role will oversee Nashville PBS areas of support including membership and on\-air / digital campaigns; major donors; individual donor recruitment; foundation and institutional giving; grant proposals and reporting; corporate sponsorship; special project and content funding; planned giving and events. This is a critical role in developing new and expanded groups of leadership and legacy givers and grantors based on the organization’s needs and desires in serving the community throughout the viewing region by expanding content offerings and strategic education and engagement. We are seeking a strategic leader with a proven relationship building, analytical and metric\-driven approach. The CDO will help build the brand’s reputation across the region while creating and recruiting a high\-performing team and fostering a collaborative culture. This position requires strategic fundraising expertise and leadership that will provide entrepreneurial, results\-driven strategy and leadership to staff by developing and implementing a program aimed at cultivating deeper relationships with community members, audiences, donors, clients, sponsors, and funders built on the trusted Nashville PBS brand. Building upon the strategic vision established in its recent multi\-year plan, Nashville PBS is executing next\-phase initiatives to launch the organization into a dynamic new era of public media. By heavily investing in cross\-platform digital distribution \& marketing, local content innovation, and an expanded regional footprint, the station has unified its operational goals. Provide strategic fundraising expertise and leadership by serving as a collaborative in\-house resource on nonprofit fund development, marketing and communication best practices, emerging revenue trends, and opportunities forgrowth.

Work with the CEO and the Senior Team to provide leadership and collaborative support to all Nashville PBS staff.

Collaborate with other team members to foster a positive and productive culture and contribute toward the overallgrowth of Nashville PBS, within our mission, vision and values.
