new_jobs.title: Director of Development
new_jobs.description:
**POSITION TITLE:** Director of Development

**WORK SCHEDULE:** Daytime, evenings, and some weekends

**COUNTY:** Kern County

**OFFICE LOCATION:** Kern County \& Fresno County, CA

**SUPERVISORS:** President \& CEO

**SALARY:** $72,800 – 83,200 annually

**TYPE:** Full\-time, Exempt

**Organization Overview:**   
Big Brothers Big Sisters of Central California (BBBSCC) is a nationally recognized, award\-winning agency dedicated to igniting the potential in young people by creating and supporting one\-to\-one mentoring relationships. Serving Madera, Fresno, Tulare, and Kings counties. Our mission is to help children realize their full potential through mentorship, and we are looking for a dynamic Development Director to help sustain and grow our impact.

**Position Summary:**  
The Development Director is a key leadership role responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive fundraising strategy to support and expand BBBSCC’s programs. This role oversees donor relations, grant writing, corporate partnerships, event planning, and overall fundraising efforts. The ideal candidate is a strategic thinker with a passion for youth development and a proven track record of securing funding in the nonprofit sector.

**Key Responsibilities:** 

***Fundraising \& Donor Development***

* Develop and implement an annual fundraising strategy to meet and exceed revenue goals.
* Cultivate and maintain relationships with individual donors, corporate sponsors, and community partners.
* Manage donor stewardship activities, ensuring consistent engagement and recognition.
* Expand the major gifts program through research, cultivation, and solicitation of high\-net\-worth individuals.
* Develop and oversee an effective planned giving program.

***Grants \& Corporate Partnerships***

* Research and identify grant opportunities; write and manage grant proposals and reports.
* Build and maintain relationships with corporate sponsors, securing sponsorships for events and initiatives.
* Oversee compliance and reporting for all secured funding.

***Special Events \& Campaigns***

* Plan, execute, and evaluate major fundraising events, such as Bowl for Kids’ Sake, 300 Club, and other community\-driven initiatives.
* Lead annual giving campaigns, including direct mail and digital fundraising strategies.
* Partner with marketing to develop compelling fundraising materials and donor communications.

***Leadership \& Strategy***

* Work closely with the President \& CEO, Board of Directors, and Development Committee to align fundraising efforts with organizational goals.
* Provide leadership in financial forecasting related to fundraising efforts.
* Maintain donor databases, tracking engagement and impact metrics.

**Qualifications:** 

* Bachelor’s degree in nonprofit management, business, communications, related field OR 5\+ years of experience in nonprofit fundraising, development, or donor relations.
* Proven success in securing major gifts, grants, and corporate sponsorships.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills, with experience in grant writing.
* Excellent relationship\-building skills with donors, sponsors, and community leaders.
* Knowledge of donor management systems (e.g., Aplos \& GiveSmart) is a plus.
* Ability to work flexible hours, including some evenings and weekends for events.

**Compensation:** 

* Compensation salary ranging from $72,800 – 83,200 annually.
* Mileage for use of a personal vehicle for work\-related travel will be reimbursed at the current federal rate.
* Company cell phone will be provided to conduct business calls, text, and email.
* Accumulation of Paid Time Off (PTO) equal to 11 paid holidays and 2 weeks’ paid vacation.
* 100% Vision and Dental and 80% medical health care cover.
* Life insurance.
* Retirement plan with company match.

**How To Apply:**

* Submit a cover letter, resume, and three professional references to info@bigs.org. Applications will be accepted until the position is filled.
* BBBSCC is an equal\-opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

**For more information:**

Big Brothers Big Sisters \| 4047 N Fresno Street \| Fresno, CA 93726 www.bigs.org

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $72,800\.00 \- $83,200\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Retirement plan
* Vision insurance

Education:

* Bachelor's (Preferred)

Experience:

* Non\-profit Fundraising: 5 years (Preferred)

License/Certification:

* Driver's License (Required)

Willingness to travel:

* 25% (Required)

Work Location: In person

new_jobs.title: Deputy Director of Institutional Giving
new_jobs.description:
**Employment Type:**  

Full\-Time/Exempt **Wage:**  

$115,000 \- $125,000 base pay **Department:**  

Philanthropy **Reports To:**  

Director of Institutional Giving **Position Location**  

California – Oakland, Los Angeles, Sacramento, Riverside, Fresno **Work Schedule:**  

Four Day Work Week: Two (2\) days in office, two (2\) days remote. However, there may be certain circumstances, like pressing deadlines or peak business periods, where it becomes necessary for the organization to temporarily switch to a five\-day work week. **WHY THIS ROLE, WHY NOW**


California will define the next decade of climate action, and the resources behind that work have to keep pace. EnviroVoters is scaling its institutional funding program, and we need a senior fundraiser who can lead people, own strategy, and represent the organization with our most important foundation partners. This role sits at the center of a growing team, shaping how we cultivate funders, build the pipeline, and turn ambitious climate and justice campaigns into sustained investment.


This is a leadership role. The Deputy Director of Institutional Giving manages grant staff, drives portfolio strategy across both EnviroVoters (501c4\) and the EnviroVoters Education Fund (501c3\), and serves as a senior relationship holder with institutional funders. If you have led teams, closed six\-figure grants, and want your work to fuel real political power, we want to hear from you.

**What Makes This Role Distinctive**

* Four\-day work week for most of the year with five\-day weeks during peak periods
* Management of a growing institutional giving team, with authority to shape systems, strategy, and portfolio assignments
* Ownership of a portfolio of the organization's most significant institutional funders, with direct responsibility for cultivation, solicitation, and negotiation
* A leadership seat in departmental planning, revenue forecasting, and annual budgeting
* Dual\-entity fundraising across c3 and c4 structures, work that few institutional giving roles offer
* Comprehensive benefits package, including health, vision and dental coverage, 401(k) with employer match,12 days PTO, 12 holidays, a year\-end office closure between Christmas and New Year, and additional benefits that support employee well\-being and work\-life balance

**Position Summary**


As a senior member of the Philanthropy Team, the Deputy Director of Institutional Giving leads the day\-to\-day operation and growth of the institutional giving program for EnviroVoters and the Ed Fund. This role manages grant staff and consultants, owns a portfolio of high\-priority institutional funders, drives annual grant strategy and revenue planning in partnership with the Director of Institutional Giving, and ensures the full grant lifecycle runs with excellence across both entities.

**KEY PRIORITIES**

*Lead and develop the institutional giving team*

* Manage grant staff and consultants supporting the institutional giving portfolio, including hiring, coaching, and performance development
* Set portfolio assignments, workflows, and quality standards across proposals, reports, and stewardship activities
* Evaluate and strengthen systems and processes for grants management, ensuring compliance and alignment with organizational values

*Own strategic funder relationships*

* Manage a portfolio of the organization's largest and most complex institutional funders, leading cultivation, solicitation, negotiation, and stewardship
* Represent EnviroVoters and the Ed Fund with funders and program officers, including independently leading meetings, pitches, and site visits

*Drive portfolio strategy and revenue growth*

* Support in the execution of an annual institutional giving strategy aligned with organizational priorities and revenue goals
* Expand the funder pipeline by identifying, researching, and cultivating new institutional prospects for both entities
* Support the Director of Institutional Giving in annual budgeting, revenue forecasting, and monthly revenue reporting

*Ensure grant management excellence across both entities*

* Oversee production of multi\-year and one\-year proposals and reports, ensuring quality, accuracy, and timely delivery
* Provide internal guidance on permissible grant usage across c3 and c4 entities, including compliance with lobbying and electoral activity restrictions
* Project manage complex, cross\-departmental proposal development and ensure funding agreements are finalized, signed, and paid on schedule
* Work proactively with program, policy, and finance staff to meet all programmatic and fiscal reporting requirements

**What We Need** **From** **You**

* Seven or more years of institutional fundraising experience in a nonprofit environment, including demonstrated success securing and renewing five\- and six\-figure grants
* Two or more years of staff management experience, with a track record of coaching and developing fundraising or grants professionals
* Expertise in cultivating and stewarding external funder relationships, including leading solicitations and negotiations independently
* Working knowledge of 501(c)(3\) and 501(c)(4\) fundraising distinctions, or demonstrated ability to learn and apply them quickly
* Deep understanding of the full grant lifecycle and grant writing craft, with the ability to edit and elevate the work of others
* Ability to manage multiple proposals, deadlines, staff, and stakeholders simultaneously under pressure
* Skilled in persuasive written and oral communication, with the ability to synthesize complex policy work into compelling cases for support
* Exceptional attention to detail, discretion, and ability to maintain confidentiality
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office; experience with Raiser's Edge/RENXT or similar CRM preferred
* Passionate about environmental protection, racial justice, and meaningful social change

**Additional Strengths We Welcome**

* Experience fundraising for advocacy, electoral, o

new_jobs.description:
**Careers** **by** **E****mpowering Futures, Building Communities**

Imagine going to work each day knowing that your efforts positively impact individuals and communities. As a global movement and the nation's leading nonprofit organization dedicated to strengthening communities through youth development, healthy living, and social responsibility, the YMCA of Greater San Francisco offers more than just a job—we offer a career with a future and the opportunity to make a lasting difference in your community. At the Y, you can uncover your passion and build a lifelong career addressing some of the nation's most pressing social issues.  


**Our****Organizational** **Culture**

At the YMCA of Greater San Francisco, we are committed to demonstrating values that aim to building strong communities where you can **Be**, **Belong** and **Become.** With **Truth \& Courage**, we strive to understand and act on individual and societal truths. We ensure a safe and inclusive environment with **Authenticity \& Accessibility**, allowing everyone to participate according to their needs. Our approach is **Dependable** **\& Creative**, as we respond to community needs through strong partnerships. Above all, we embody **Dignity \& Empathy**, treating everyone with respect and compassion, and recognizing the inherent dignity in all individuals.

**Qualifications**


* 21\+ years old; 3\+ years of experience in youth programs with supervisory responsibilities.
* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in youth development or coaching.
* Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.
* Proficiency with MS Office Suite.
* Availability Monday through Friday, with flexibility to work evenings and weekends as needed. This position is in\-person.
* Experience working with individuals from diverse backgrounds, including but not limited to ethnic, socioeconomic, educational, and religious diversity.
* Familiarity with schools and community\-based organizations.
* Fingerprint and TB test clearance, YMCA\-approved First Aid, CPR, and AED certification.

  

**Preferred Qualifications (Optional)**


* Experience working within SFUSD school system.
* Bilingual in Spanish, Cantonese, or Mandarin.
* Credits for After\-School \& CA State Licensing experience; Y\-USA Team Leader Certification
* Credentialed educator
* Youth Development Trainee
* STEAM program experience
* Salesforce software experience



**Position Summary**

  


The **Youth Program Director I** oversees the planning, development, coordination, and management of out\-of\-school time programs for elementary\-aged youth at the Mission Family YMCA. These programs include after school programming, summer day camp or Power Scholars Academy, and STEAM\-focused initiatives. The Director ensures the delivery of high\-quality, engaging, and inclusive experiences for participants while maintaining compliance with program standards and partner expectations.



The position is responsible for staff supervision, operational oversight, and partnership engagement. This includes supporting and coaching program staff, fostering strong relationships with school administrators, families, community partners, and YMCA colleagues. The Director works collaboratively with senior leadership to align programs with organizational goals and youth development strategies.



Key responsibilities include fiscal oversight (budgeting and contract administration), participating in strategic initiatives within the Youth Development POD, and contributing to the YMCA’s Annual Giving Campaign. The role also serves as a liaison with public and private partners in support of the YMCA’s mission and values.



The position requires adaptability, strong organizational skills, and a commitment to creating a safe, supportive, and enriching environment for all participants and staff.

  

**Program Development \& Management**


* Oversee planning, supervision, and daily operations of Out of School Time programs, including after school care, summer day camp, and out\-of\-school time day camps at an elementary school site.
* Ensure smooth program implementation and alignment with YMCA of San Francisco’s strategic vision, emphasizing safety, quality, and fiscal stewardship.
* Coordinate and support day\-to\-day programming, including educational excursions and activity schedules in partnership with instructors and specialists.
* Design and maintain a safe, inclusive, and welcoming program environment for all participants and staff.
* Develop enrichment and academic activities that meet the Youth Development Principles of the YMCA and partner organizations.
* Incorporate feedback from youth, families, and staff into program planning and curriculum.
* Guide staff in delivering culturally responsive, developmentally appropriate, and engaging activities for children of diverse backgrounds.
* Ensure all programming adheres to child safety protocols and YMCA standards.

  

**Participant Supervision \& Safety**


* Monitor participant well\-being and maintain required staff\-to\-student supervision ratios at all times.
* Identify and respond to incidents and emergencies following YMCA protocols.
* Implement and model consistent safety and behavior guidelines across all programs.
* Ensure all programs comply with safety, risk management, and mandated reporting policies.

* Support alignment between team practices and the organization’s youth development strategies.
* Foster strong working relationships among staff, participants, volunteers, and community stakeholders.

new_jobs.title: Executive Assistant to the CEO
new_jobs.description:
**About Canvas Institute**

Canvas Institute is a Staten Island\-based nonprofit that has served over 60,000 youth and residents through school programming and community engagement. Our work spans arts and culture, youth development, civic engagement, and health and wellness, including our Compassionate Systems social\-emotional learning programming delivered across more than 30 New York City Public Schools. We are a licensed NYC Public Schools vendor, a Project Pivot Partner, and an NYC Civic Corps (AmeriCorps) host site.

**Position Summary**

The CEO is looking for a highly organized, technology\-savvy, and proactive professional to serve as Executive Assistant to the CEO while also keeping the office and day\-to\-day operations running smoothly and providing administrative support across the organization’s projects and grants. This is a hands\-on, multi\-faceted role that blends three areas of work: directly supporting the CEO, managing the office and its administrative systems, and serving as the administrative backbone for the programs, projects, and funded initiatives Canvas Institute takes on.

The right person can anticipate needs, stay several steps ahead, juggle competing priorities, and handle sensitive organizational matters with discretion and follow\-through. Canvas Institute is building an AI\-first team, and the ideal candidate is fluent with digital tools and knows how to leverage AI to work faster and smarter. We are looking for a problem\-solver who takes ownership and gets things done without being asked twice.

**Key ResponsibilitiesExecutive Support to the CEO**

**Schedule and calendar management**

* Build and maintain the CEO’s calendar in Google Calendar, including meetings, program visits, community events, and travel
* Protect the CEO’s time by prioritizing requests and resolving scheduling conflicts
* Prepare the CEO for each day with agendas, materials, and reminders

**Direct support and correspondence**

* Track the CEO’s tasks, deadlines, and follow\-ups, and keep everything organized in one place
* Manage correspondence, including drafting, sending, and routing important documents and letters
* Handle time\-sensitive matters such as mailing official correspondence
* Coordinate appointments and administrative needs on the CEO’s behalf
* Serve as a dependable point of contact who keeps the CEO’s professional logistics running smoothly

**Office Management**

* Keep the office running day to day, ensuring it is organized, stocked, and ready for staff, students, and visitors
* Order and manage office supplies, program materials, and equipment, and track what has been purchased
* Serve as the point of contact for vendors, deliveries, building needs, and basic facilities coordination
* Maintain organized files and records across Google Workspace, both digital and physical, and keep shared drives and folders structured and up to date
* Establish and maintain simple office systems and routines that help the whole team stay organized
* Provide general administrative support to staff and help keep internal operations moving

**Errands and on\-the\-ground support**

* Run organizational errands on the CEO’s and the office’s behalf, including trips to the post office, bank, and other locations
* Pick up and deliver documents, packages, and materials as needed
* Provide reliable transportation for errands and time\-sensitive tasks

**Project and Grant Administration**

Canvas Institute regularly takes on funded programs, partnerships, and grant\-supported projects that require careful paperwork, tracking, and reporting. This role serves as the administrative lead that keeps those projects compliant, documented, and on track behind the scenes.

* Serve as the administrative point person for the organization’s programs, projects, and grants, handling the paperwork and documentation required on behalf of Canvas Institute
* Complete, submit, and file the forms and paperwork tied to funded programs and partnerships, and make sure deadlines are met
* Track participant information for programs such as youth employment and workforce initiatives, including enrollment paperwork, attendance, time and hours, and required documentation
* Support partnership and civic engagement projects by completing required paperwork, coordinating logistics, and documenting trainings and activities
* Maintain complete, well\-organized records for each project, ensuring everything is also captured and backed up digitally in the right folders
* Assist with data collection, reporting, and documenting outcomes and processes for projects, grants, and funded programs
* Keep project files audit\-ready so that documentation can be produced quickly when funders or partners request it

**Required Qualifications**

* Valid driver’s license and access to a reliable personal vehicle (required, both are essential to this role)
* Strong technology skills and hands\-on experience with Google Calendar and Google Workspace (Gmail, Docs, Drive, Sheets)
* Comfort and familiarity with AI tools, with the ability to leverage AI to complete tasks efficiently (Canvas uses Claude and ChatGPT internally)
* Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities, projects, and deadlines at once
* Attention to detail and accuracy, especially with paperwork, recordkeeping, and documentation
* Excellent written and verbal communication
* High level of discretion, professionalism, and trustworthiness with confidential matters
* Proactive, resourceful, and able to work independently with minimal direction
* Availability for a flexible schedule, including occasional evenings or weekends tied to events

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Prior experience as an executive assistant, office manager, administrative coordinator, or in a comparable support role
* Experience supporting grant\-funded or program\-based work, including paperwork, compliance, recordkeeping, or reporting
* Familiarity with nonprofit or comm

new_jobs.title: Director of Development
new_jobs.description:
**Dominican Academy**  
Since 1897, Dominican Academy has been educating young women in the Catholic tradition of Saint Dominic, offering a rigorous academic program distinguished by a firm commitment to values of truth, integrity, prayer, community, and service. As a Catholic, all\-honors high school sponsored by Dominican Veritas Ministries and located on the Upper East Side of Manhattan, Dominican Academy challenges and empowers young women to become spiritual, intellectual, moral, and socially responsible leaders in a global society. Dominican Academy is proud of its current association with Dominican Veritas Ministries, a broad coalition of Dominican\-sponsored ministries.

**Position Overview:**  
Reporting directly to the President, the Director of Development leads the planning, structure, and implementation of the school's expansive development program. Well versed in D.A.'s mission and the lived experience of the Four Pillars, the Director provides strategic direction, carries a personal portfolio of major donors, and serves as the school's spokesperson in its philanthropic efforts.

The Director works in close partnership with the President on major\-donor strategy, collaborates with the Communications team on the annual report, alumnae magazine, and appeals, and builds the long\-range advancement plan hand in hand with the Vice President for Finance.

The team you will lead:

**Development Database \& Annual Fund Manager**: Owns gift processing, database integrity, and the annual fund engine that keeps the program running.

**Alumnae Relations \& Special Events Manager**: Leads alumnae engagement and the school's signature events, including the Spring Auction \& Benefit.

**Grant Writing Consultant**: Supports foundation research, proposal writing, and grant follow\-up under the Director's direction.

**Primary Responsibilities:**

**Strategic Vision \& Planning**

● Partner with the President to set the vision, goals, and priorities of D.A.’s mission advancement efforts.  
● Build a long\-range plan and annual goals for the Development Office, in close coordination with the VP for Finance.  
● Establish KPIs and metrics to track progress against school\-wide fundraising goals.  
● Prepare and manage the Development Office budget and report progress to the President and Board.  
● Serve as a trusted liaison to the Development Committee Chair and Board of Trustees.

**Team Leadership \& Operations**

● Lead all aspects of the development plan: major gifts, annual appeal, grant writing, alumnae engagement, and events.  
● Supervise, evaluate, and develop Development Office team members.  
● Ensure accurate gift recording, timely acknowledgment, and effective use of the donor database.  
● Oversee foundation giving, including grant writing, budgets, funder contact, and follow up.

**Community \& Engagement**

● Direct programming for alumnae engagement and the school’s special events.  
● Collaborate with Communications on the annual report, alumnae magazine, and appeals.  
● Be a present, engaged presence at school\-wide events as an ambassador for the mission.  
● Work collaboratively across offices and with faculty to support school initiatives.

Successful candidates will work collegially with colleagues, serve as ambassadors of all\-girls Catholic education in the Dominican tradition, and possess the listening and storytelling skills to understand and communicate donors’ values and commitment to DA. Professionalism, thoughtful attention to confidential information, effective communication skills, and prioritization of complex, demanding work are essential qualities for the Director of Development.

**The Ideal Candidate: A Forward\-Facing Leader with Real Hustle**

● A closer, not just a cultivator. A forward\-facing presence with genuine hustle, who converts relationships and warm leads into gifts and follows through to the ask.

If you answered ‘yes’, to any of the above, World Relief, and millions of people around the world need you.  

  

At this pivotal moment in time, we are rapidly expanding and growing our team to meet the increasing needs of our world. We are looking for people who want to use their gifts and talents to make a real and tangible difference in our world and the lives of the suffering.

**Organization Overview**

The original Freedom Quilting Bee (Bee), was a women’s quilting cooperative established in 1966 during the tumultuous times of the Civil Rights Movement. The cooperative manufactured quilts for the largest retailers in America; Sears, Bloomingdale’s and interior designers in New York City.

***Our Mission \- To teach, share and preserve threads of quilting arts, resistance \& legacy of Black women in Gee’s Bend and Alberta, Alabama.***

***Our Vision \- To inspire makers across the globe, sharing threads of transformative art, culture, and the pioneering legacy of Black women in Gee’s Bend and Alberta, Alabama.*** 

Freedom Quilting Bee Legacy (Legacy) was founded to ensure the heroic, historic, innovative, artistic and incredible odds\-beating work of the women of the original Bee isn’t lost to history. We celebrate and teach about the work and accomplishments of those women (and men) past and present who made the Bee such a powerful artistic and community force. We will offer real economic opportunities for a new generation of quilt makers and artisans.

We provide solutions to community needs, such as Internet access, adult learning and skills building.

We draw tourism to the area and offer a deeper experience of the history and current activities, with shops, lodging, classes and interactive exhibits.

We preserve the past and evolve into the future by creating jobs, building wealth and teaching life skills to improve our community and its people..

Our nonprofit organization is dedicated to making a positive impact on our community by providing outreach, access to history and tourism. We rely heavily on fundraising to sustain our programs and services, and we are seeking a Director of Development to lead our fundraising efforts.

*Student U is an education nonprofit rooted in Durham.* ***We are students, families, and educators cultivating joy and academic excellence in learning communities so all students thrive in Durham and beyond.***  


*We believe everyone deserves opportunities in their educational journey that enable them to reach their full potential. This is not yet the reality for every student. Structural racism, intergenerational poverty, and other injustices create significant barriers for first\-generation college\-bound students and entire communities across Durham and the U.S., including here in Durham, N.C.*  


*We support students and their families inside and outside of school, helping them overcome challenges to reach college graduation. At the same time, we work to improve the public education system by sharing successful strategies with schools and educators and building a strong community at The W.G. Pearson Center, aiming for a better future for our youth.**We show up for students and families with joy, rigor, and deep belief that every person is worthy of opportunities that help them grow and thrive.*
*That same culture extends to our team. We are a collaborative, growth\-minded staff that invests in each other the same way we invest in students. We celebrate wins, learn from challenges, and are committed to building an organization where everyone can do the best work of their career. If you are looking for a place where your skills will matter, your voice will be heard, and your work will directly change lives in Durham and across North Carolina, you have found it.*


**POSITION OVERVIEW**



Student U has grown from a $93,000 budget in 2007 to an estimated $6\.1M budget in 2026, and we are ready for the next chapter. We are building a robust individual major gifts program while continuing to deepen and diversify our relationships with foundations, government funders, and corporate partners. This is a rare opportunity to join a high\-performing advancement team at an inflection point and help shape what comes next.



The Development Director will serve as a key leader on the advancement team, reporting directly to the Chief Advancement Officer and working in close partnership with the Executive Director. This is a relationship\-centered, externally\-facing role for someone who is energized by connecting people to meaningful work and has the experience to translate those relationships into long\-term investment in our mission. The Development Director will own and lead Student U’s Major Gifts program, carrying a personal portfolio of donors at the 5\-figure gift level while also serving as a key partner to the Executive Director in managing relationships with our top 20 donors.

* Carry and grow a personal portfolio of major donors and prospects at the 5\-figure gift level, developing individualized engagement strategies to secure one\-time and multi\-year commitments
* Serve as a strategic partner to the Executive Director in managing relationships with Student U’s top 20 donors, providing preparation, follow\-through, and relationship continuity to support the Executive Director’s caseload
* Lead outreach, cultivation, and stewardship across your portfolio with warmth, intentionality, and consistent follow\-through
* Design and implement a meaningful stewardship program that keeps current donors connected, informed, and inspired by their impact on Student U students and families

* Develop and implement a tiered recurring giving program to grow sustainable individual revenue
* Work closely with the Board of Directors to deepen board giving, strengthen board member engagement in fundraising, and build a culture of philanthropy at the governance level

  


**Foundations, Government, and Corporate Relationships (15%)**
* Support the growth of a portfolio of foundation, government, and corporate relationships, serving as a strategic thought partner
* Collaborate with the Director of Partnerships and the Grants Committee to ensure proposals are timely, accurate, compelling, and aligned with Student U's work and funder priorities
* Represent Student U at site visits, community events, and funder convenings

  


**Development Operations and Collaboration (15%)**
* Partner with the Data Manager to ensure the donor database (Salesforce) is maintained with accuracy and integrity, and that donor acknowledgment is prompt and meaningful
* Coordinate with the Chief Advancement Officer and Director of Finance on revenue forecasting and cash flow projections
* Bring a continuous improvement mindset to development systems, strategies, and processes, identifying opportunities to strengthen infrastructure and outcomes
* Serve as an engaged collaborator across teams, particularly with the program team, to maintain a clear and compelling picture of Student U's impact for donors

 **REQUIREMENTS \& KEY COMPETENCIES**



You are a seasoned development professional who leads with relationships and follows through with results. You know how to build authentic connections with donors and move them from curiosity to commitment, and you understand that keeping a donor is just as important as finding one. The Director of Development (DD) leads the strategy, execution, and performance of all fundraising efforts that support the overall mission and growth of the Boys \& Girls Clubs of Muncie (BGCM). The DD is also responsible for overseeing marketing and communications to ensure alignment with organizational goals and to strengthen visibility, engagement, and donor support.

Description

Mission: To inspire and enable all young people, especially those who need us most to reach their full potential as productive, caring, and responsible citizens.

FLSA Classification: Exempt

Salary Range: $48,000 \- $58,000

**BOYS \& GIRLS CLUBS OF MIDDLE TENNESSEE**  

**Executive Director – Clarksville Unit****Reports To** Chief Executive Officer **Location** Clarksville, Tennessee

**ABOUT BOYS \& GIRLS CLUBS OF MIDDLE TENNESSEE**  

Boys \& Girls Clubs of Middle Tennessee (BGCMT) enables all young people—especially those who need us most—to reach their full potential as productive, caring and responsible citizens. Through safe, high\-quality Club experiences, BGCMT helps young people build the academic, social, leadership and career skills needed to thrive.  

Our work is guided by the **Five Readys \= Future Ready:** Ready to Read, Ready for Class, Ready to Graduate, Ready for Career and Ready to Thrive in Community.  

**THE OPPORTUNITY**  

BGCMT is launching its first Clarksville Club at Kenwood Middle School. This is a unique opportunity for a community executive and hands\-on operator to help build a strong, sustainable model from the ground up. The immediate priority is to launch and stabilize an excellent Club experience at Kenwood, establish trusted partnerships and demonstrate clear value to youth, families, the school system and the broader community.  

The guiding philosophy for this role is simple: nail the model first, then scale it. Expansion will become a greater focus in Year Two only after the Kenwood model is stable, successful and positioned for responsible growth.  

**POSITION SUMMARY**  

The Executive Director – Clarksville Unit serves as the day\-to\-day leader and primary liaison for Boys \& Girls Clubs of Middle Tennessee’s work in Clarksville and Montgomery County. Reporting to the Chief Executive Officer, this position is responsible for successfully launching and stabilizing the Kenwood Middle School Club, leading local staff and operations, building strong school and community relationships, and helping establish the financial and organizational foundation needed for long\-term impact.  

The Executive Director will serve as a key partner to the CEO and will work closely with the Clarksville Advisory Council. The position will develop and maintain productive day\-to\-day relationships with school leaders, community organizations, corporations, government partners, funders, families and other stakeholders who value BGCMT’s services and want to see them succeed and grow.  

This is a builder\-operator role requiring equal strength in community engagement, staff leadership, operational execution, partnership development and youth\-centered program quality.

**Program Quality and Youth Outcomes**

* Ensure programming is responsive to student interests, family needs and community priorities.
* Establish measurable goals for youth participation, engagement, learning and developmental outcomes.
* Create consistent opportunities for youth voice, family engagement, recognition and monthly signature events.

**Staff Leadership and Culture**  

* Recruit, select, onboard, supervise and develop a high\-performing local team.
* Build a culture grounded in youth safety, accountability, inclusion, collaboration and continuous improvement.
* Provide regular coaching, feedback, performance management and professional development.
* Ensure adequate staffing coverage and strong execution during all hours of Club operation.
* Develop internal leadership capacity as the Clarksville model matures.

**School, Community, Corporate and Government Partnerships**

**About Dog Tag**



Dog Tag is a nonprofit social enterprise that combines our one\-of\-a\-kind Fellowship program, which serves veterans with service\-connected disabilities, military spouses, and caregivers nationwide, with a high\-quality bakery. Based in Washington, DC’s Georgetown neighborhood, Dog Tag provides an innovative, hands\-on business education program that equips Fellows with the skills to launch their own businesses, transition to meaningful careers in the civilian workforce, or continue their educational priorities.



The bakery is where delicious treats and meaningful service meet. Every item we create reflects our dedication to supporting those who have served our country, and every sale enables us to serve more Fellows and alumni. The ability to engage with us in our cafe, as well as through local catering and gift boxes shipped nationwide, extends the reach of our mission to every service member family seeking renewed purpose, identity, and commitment.

Delta Health is seeking a visionary, collaborative, and mission\-driven **Chief Executive Officer (CEO)** to lead our organization into its next chapter. This is an exceptional opportunity to make a meaningful impact in a rural community while guiding a financially patient\-centered healthcare organization committed to **Excellence, Every Patient, Every Time.**



Reporting directly to the Board of Directors, the CEO serves as the organization's senior executive leader and is responsible for strategic direction, operational excellence, financial stewardship, physician partnerships, workforce engagement, and community relationships.


**What You'll Do**



As CEO, you will:


* Lead the development and execution of the organization's strategic vision.
* Foster a culture focused on quality, safety, accountability, and exceptional patient care.
* Ensure the financial strength and long\-term sustainability of the organization.
* Build strong partnerships with physicians, employees, the Board of Directors, and community stakeholders.
* Champion operational excellence, innovation, and continuous improvement.
* Ensure compliance with all regulatory and accreditation requirements.
* Support leadership development while promoting an engaged and high\-performing workforce.
* Represent Delta Health throughout the community and strengthen regional healthcare partnerships.


**What We're Looking For**



The ideal candidate will bring:


* 7\-10\+ years of rural and independent healthcare leadership experience.
* Previous CEO, COO, or senior executive leadership experience in a hospital or health system preferred.
* Master's degree in Healthcare Administration (MHA), Business Administration (MBA), or related field required.
* Bachelor's degree in Healthcare Administration, Business, or related discipline.
* Demonstrated financial acumen with knowledge of healthcare reimbursement and hospital operations.
* Experience working collaboratively with governing boards, medical staff, and executive leadership.
* Outstanding communication, relationship\-building, and leadership skills.
* Strong strategic thinking, data\-driven decision making, and emotional intelligence.
* FACHE certification preferred.
* ***As a special district it is expected that the candidate will reside in Delta County.***


**Why Delta Health?**



At Delta Health, you'll find more than a leadership opportunity—you'll find a community.



Located on Colorado's beautiful Western Slope, Delta offers an exceptional quality of life with year\-round outdoor recreation, affordable living, outstanding schools, and easy access to Grand Mesa National Forest, Black Canyon of the Gunnison National Park, world\-class skiing, hiking, mountain biking, fishing, and hunting.



As our next CEO, you'll have the opportunity to:


* Shape the future of healthcare in a growing rural community.
* Lead an engaged executive team and dedicated workforce.
* Build lasting partnerships with physicians and community leaders.
* Drive innovation while preserving the personalized care that defines rural healthcare.
* Leave a lasting legacy for generations of patients and families.


**Join Our Team**



If you are an authentic leader with a passion for rural healthcare, strategic growth, operational excellence, and community engagement, we encourage you to apply.


**Delta Health is an Equal Opportunity Employer.** We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where every employee can thrive.

**Reports to:** President/CEO

**ORGANIZATION PROFILE:**

Historic Charleston Foundation (HCF) is a non\-profit organization advancing the broad mission of historic preservation and protecting the livability of the community. As historic preservation has evolved into more than a movement to simply save historic buildings, the Foundation has broadened its scope into balancing the needs of modern society with protecting the significant cultural fabric of our historic communities. The Foundation’s three main mission pillars are Preservation, Advocacy, and Museums.

**POSITION SUMMARY:**

The Chief Philanthropy Officer (CPO) is accountable for the overall management and success of a well\-rounded Philanthropy Department that addresses all private fundraising (annual, major, planned), fundraising related events and sponsorships, grants, and volunteer engagement. The successful candidate will collaborate with the CEO, executive team, volunteer committees, and the Board of Trustees to plan and execute resource development strategies and measure performance against goals. The ideal candidate will partner with colleagues, community groups, and other stakeholders, lead complex projects, and serve as the organization’s champion among key donor constituencies. The Chief Philanthropy Officer reports directly to the President \& CEO.

**JOB RESPONSIBILITIES:**

**Leadership**

* Lead, mentor, and supervise a Philanthropy team of three additional full\-time professionals
* Collaborate with the CEO, other Department Heads, and Board of Trustees in all philanthropic activities to fund the organization’s operations and deliver programs within the community
* Create and manage an effective annual plan for a fully realized Philanthropy Department that supports the organization’s three mission areas. Execute an objective, measurable plan in coordination with the HCF team, consultants, and volunteers
* Collaborate with the Foundation’s marketing and communications team as well as external marketing partners

**Revenue Generation**

* Lead all efforts to meet and/or exceed annual revenue goals as determined by strategic planning efforts, organizational needs, and proper assessment of philanthropic readiness
* Directly manage and advance a major gift portfolio
* Oversee efforts to create, execute, and evaluate key events directly impacting the Philanthropy Department’s fundraising goals to cultivate and steward leadership donors to HCF and generate operating revenue
* Direct the overall donor and prospect pipeline to ensure ongoing acquisition, retention, growth

**Revenue Forecasting**

* Develop and maintain monthly and annual revenue forecasts
* Develop and implement an annual resource development plan that includes long\- and short\-term objectives for the philanthropy department with measurable revenue targets
* Participate in the development, implementation, and monitoring of the organization’s philanthropic development activities, through controlling expenditures within budget and maintaining donor and financial records

**Community Engagement**

**Chief Executive Officer \| Occlunova**
========================================


**Location:** USA, with a preference for Minnesota or New Jersey, with regular travel to clinical sites and occasional travel to NLC Health Ventures in Amsterdam


**Commitment:** Full\-time


**Compensation:** Meaningful equity participation \+ competitive compensation


**Reports to:** Board of Directors


**About the Venture – Occlunova**
---------------------------------



Occlunova is a medtech venture developing the next generation of Left Atrial Appendage Occlusion (LAAO) technology to prevent stroke in patients with atrial fibrillation (AF). AF affects more than 60 million people worldwide and drives a 5x increase in stroke risk. Yet, existing LAAO devices remain limited by leak rates, traumatic anchoring mechanisms, and persistent device\-related thrombosis.



Occlunova's platform combines an anatomy\-conforming balloon with an atraumatic anchoring method engineered to reduce leaks, minimize procedural risk, and let AF patients safely come off oral anticoagulant therapy. The company is also integrating left atrial appendage electrical isolation (LAAEI) into the same device, enabling occlusion and ablation to be performed in a single procedure \- an increasingly relevant combination with no direct equivalent on the market today.



The technology has been validated in benchtop, ex\-vivo (human cadaver), and in\-vivo (canine) studies that indicate measurable advantages in comparison with market technologies.



Occlunova has raised its pre\-seed and is preparing for seed financing to fund its first\-in\-human studies in 2027\. All major structural heart strategics are active acquirers in this space, and pre\-approval exits are the norm rather than the exception.



Occlunova is part of the venture portfolio of NLC Health Ventures, built in collaboration with Fusion MedTech and clinical collaborators at Mayo Clinic.


**The Opportunity**
-------------------



We are looking for an experienced medtech CEO to take Occlunova from a validated prototype with a strong clinical and technical team in place through further clinical development and towards a strategic exit. This is a company\-building role for a leader who has done this before: someone who has taken an implantable cardiovascular device through fundraising, clinical trials, and ideally into the hands of a strategic acquirer or to market.



You will own the company's overall strategy and be the primary face of Occlunova to investors, clinical partners, and future acquirers, while building the team and organization needed to execute against an ambitious but realistic timeline.


**Your Role**
-------------


### **Set Strategy and Lead the Company**


* Own overall corporate strategy, prioritization, and resource allocation across product, clinical, and regulatory workstreams
* Translate the LAAO/LAAEI platform into a clear, milestone\-driven path to value creation, together with identifying and managing key partners like CROs, CDMOs, advisors, and other key suppliers.
* Represent Occlunova externally to investors, KOLs, strategics, and industry partners

### **Lead Fundraising**


* Drive seed and subsequent financing rounds, including investor targeting, materials, diligence, and negotiation
* Build and maintain relationships with venture investors, strategic corporate partners, and non\-dilutive funding sources
* Manage capital efficiently against the milestones that matter most for the next raise and eventual exit

### **Advance Clinical Development**


* Own the design, sequencing, and execution of clinical studies from first\-in\-human through pivotal trial
* Build relationships with interventional cardiologists, electrophysiologists, and KOLs in structural heart and AF ablation
* Work with regulatory advisors to shape a credible pathway through FDA (and OUS regulators as relevant)

### **Build Toward a Strategic Outcome**


* Understand the competitive and M\&A landscape in LAAO and PFA ablation, and position Occlunova accordingly
* Build and sustain relationships with corporate development teams at strategic acquirers
* Prepare the company operationally and financially for partnership or exit conversations

### **Build and Lead the Organization**


* Recruit and lead a high\-performing team across clinical, regulatory, engineering, and operations
* Establish the organizational structure, culture, and processes needed to scale from a small team to a clinical\- and subsequently commercial\-stage company
* Foster a culture of creativity, rigor, and perseverance, and urgency appropriate to a capital\-efficient medtech startup

**Your Profile**
----------------



We are looking for a CEO who combines deep medtech operating experience with the strategic thinking and creativity needed to build a company from this stage forward.


### **Preferred Background**


* Experience in implantable medical devices, ideally in cardiovascular or structural heart
* A track record of successfully raising capital for a medtech venture (venture capital, strategic, and/or non\-dilutive), including the financial acumen required to forecast and manage
* Experience organizing and managing clinical trials for an implantable device, from early feasibility through pivotal study
* Strong team\-building and organizational skills, with the ability to recruit and lead a multidisciplinary team

* Experience at a medtech strategic (e.g., Boston Scientific, Abbott, Medtronic, J\&J) is a plus; for example, in a business development or sales/commercial role, with an understanding of how strategics evaluate and acquire early\-stage technology

**ABOUT TGWA**

The Texas Ground Water Association is a 501(c)(6\) association for the Texas water well drilling industry. TGWA works to promote the industry and facilitate cooperation and educational opportunities among water well drillers, pump installers, manufacturers, suppliers, geoscientists, and government and scientific agencies. The organization has more than 2,500 individual members and undertakes an annual TGWA Convention each January. It is also a leading provider of in\-person and online continuing education classes for water well drillers and pump installers in the state.

Please visit us at moifa.org
  
  

Created in 1978 by the New Mexico Legislature, the Department of Cultural Affairs represents New Mexico's dedication to preserving and celebrating the cultural integrity and diversity of our state. The Department oversees a broad range of New Mexico's arts and cultural heritage agencies. These include 15 divisions representing a variety of programs and services.
  
  

The Museum of International Folk Art (MOIFA) shapes a humane world by connecting people through creative expression and artistic traditions. The Museum's holdings represent diverse cultures and constitute the largest collection of international folk art in the world. The core collection donated by Museum founder Florence Dibell Bartlett and representing 34 countries has grown to a collection of over 163,000 objects from more than 130 countries. MOIFA offers dynamic exhibitions, programs, and publications that appeal to diverse learning styles and audiences, touch the heart, engage the mind, and inspire action. We create accessible exhibitions, programming, and resources that serve the global community, including multiple perspectives that encourage further exploration of wide\-ranging subjects.
  
Why does the job exist?
  
  

As the chief operating officer, the deputy director oversees all operations at the MOIFA, including human, financial, and physical resources. Daily oversight of Museum operations by the deputy director directly impacts all facets of the Museum's mission. Responsibilities span a wide range of activities, including strategic planning, budgeting preparation, preservation of and access to collections, visitor services, staff personnel policies, and financial backup and management of facilities maintenance and repairs. The deputy director represents the Museum in the executive director's absence and as delegated by the executive director with all constituencies affiliated with the Museum.
  
How does it get done?
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
  

Museum Operations and Administration
  
  

* Assists the Executive Director in the overall administration and daily operations of the Museum.

* Assists with strategic planning, capital improvement initiatives, and operational resource management.

* Serves as principal backup to the Executive Director, ensuring continuity of leadership and operations.
* Represents the Museum with Department of Cultural Affairs leadership, governing boards, partner organizations, donors, and the public.
* Assists with strategic initiatives, special projects, contracts, and executive\-level decision making.
* Coordinates with the Executive Director to ensure alignment with organizational priorities and strategic goals.


Fiscal, Human Resources, and Administrative Management
  
  

* Assists with budget development, financial oversight, and fiscal administration.

* Develops and improves administrative procedures, financial protocols, and operational processes.
* Monitors expenditures and resource utilization, ensuring compliance with state policies and organizational objectives.
* Supports conflict resolution, organizational effectiveness, and continuous improvement initiatives.


Who are the customers?
  
  

This position serves Museum visitors, Museum staff, members, supporters, partners, friends, citizens of the state of New Mexico, with particular attention to underserved communities within our state, to help them connect with folk art traditions, including their own, as well as our national and international community of artists and institutional collaborators.

Pewabic Pottery is a 123\-year\-old arts anchor institution on Detroit’s evolving east side with a storied history, a robust identity, and an extraordinary future ahead. The ideal candidate is a strategic and ambitious leader, a compelling communicator, and an equity\-driven fundraiser who thrives on collaboration, innovation, and building meaningful partnerships to advance our mission.

**Job Title:** Senior Director of Development  

**Reports To:** CEO  

**Location:** Boston, MA; 4 days in office; 1\-day remote  

**Position Type:** Full\-Time, Salary Exempt  

**Annual Salary:** $145,000 \- $160,000

  


**Overview**:



The Senior Director of Development serves as Heading Home's lead frontline fundraiser, responsible for building meaningful relationships with individual philanthropists who are passionate about preventing and ending homelessness. This position works closely with the CEO to develop and execute strategies to identify, cultivate, solicit and steward major donors whose investments advance Heading Home's mission and long\-term strategic priorities.



This role requires a highly relationship\-driven fundraising professional with a proven ability to secure transformational gifts, develop new philanthropic partnerships, and inspire donors through compelling storytelling and measurable impact. Working closely with the CEO, Board members, and Development team, the Senior Director of Development will develop a plan to expand Heading Home's major gifts program while strengthening relationships with our most significant supporters.

  


**Key Responsibilities**

  


**Major Gift Fundraising**


* Responsible for raising at least $3M of a $6M fundraising budget.
* Maintain an active pipeline in qualified opportunities to assist in getting to the fundraising goal.
* Build and actively manage a portfolio of major gift prospects and donors.
* Develop individualized cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship strategies for each assigned donor.
* Conduct discovery meetings to identify donor interests, philanthropic priorities, and giving capacity.
* Personally solicit and close five\-, six\-, and seven\-figure gifts.
* Maintain regular communication with assigned donors through meetings, calls, correspondence, and program updates.

  


**Business Development**


* Identify, qualify, and cultivate new major gift prospects throughout Massachusetts and beyond.
* Develop relationships with leaders in financial services, healthcare, technology, life sciences, professional services, family foundations, and private philanthropy.
* Partner with Board members, volunteers, corporate partners, and community leaders to expand Heading
* Home's philanthropic network.
* Convert event attendees, volunteers, sponsors, and community partners into long\-term individual donors.

  


**Donor Engagement \& Stewardship**


* Coordinate donor visits to Heading Home programs sites.
* Create meaningful donor engagement opportunities, including CEO briefings, volunteer experiences, cultivation events, and special initiatives.
* Develop stewardship strategies that demonstrate donor impact and strengthen long\-term relationships.
* Work with Marketing \& Communications to share compelling stories of mission impact with donors.

* Oversee the development and execution of Heading Home’s two key events, Housewarming and Changemakers.
* Work closely with the CEO to motivate and engage the board and donor volunteer to meet the $3M
* Housewarming event goal.
* Manage the event vendor relationships to ensure smooth execution.

  


**Supervision and Management**


* Supervise, mentor, and develop the Grants Manager, Operations Development Associate, Volunteer Development Associate and Development Officer.  

Set team goals, monitor performance, and ensure accountability for fundraising and engagement objectives.
* Oversee daily development operations, ensuring timely execution of grants, donor stewardship, gift processing, and volunteer initiatives.
* Foster collaboration across the development team and with internal departments to support organizational fundraising goals.
* Build a high\-performing, mission\-driven team focused on continuous improvement and exceptional donor service.

  


**Data Management \& Reporting**


* Oversee the maintenance of accurate donor records, activity tracking, and moves management documentation within Raiser's Edge NXT.
* Monitor portfolio performance, donor pipeline activity, proposal success rates, and fundraising revenue.
* Prepare monthly reports and revenue forecasts for the CEO.
* Ensure all donor interactions and next steps are documented in accordance with Development Department standards.

Applicants will receive fair and impartial consideration without regard to race, sex, color, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic data, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion or other legally protected status, or any other classification protected by federal, state, or local law.

  


**Work Environment**  

Normal for an office work environment.

* Bachelor's degree required.
* Seven or more years of frontline major gift fundraising experience.
* Demonstrated success securing five\- and six\-figure philanthropic investments.
* Proven ability to independently manage and grow a major gift portfolio.
* Experience developing customized donor strategies and complex gift solicitations.
* Excellent written, verbal, and presentation skills.
* Experience with Blackbaud Raiser's Edge NXT or comparable donor CRM.
* Strong organizational and project management skills with exceptional attention to detail.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities while meeting deadlines.
* Self\-starter who works independently while collaborating effectively across teams.

* Passion for Heading Home's mission and commitment to equitable housing and ending homelessness.

**TITLE**: Director of Grants


**OVERVIEW**: The individual selected for this position will be responsible for managing a portfolio of current grant funds, ensuring proper collection of data and reporting on results for grant\-funded programs, researching and soliciting new grant funding, and providing leadership to staff responsible for managing grant\-funded programs. As a key member of the finance team, this individual will help build relationships with current and potential funders as well as help build a strong case for support in the community. The individual in this position is expected to embrace the Boys \& Girls Club of Lawrence Core Values – Diversity, Respect, Integrity, Positive Role Models, and Compassion.


**REPORTS TO**: VP of Finance \& Administration


**CLASSIFICATION**: Exempt


**BENEFITS INCLUDE**:

BGCLK’s commitment to celebrating diversity, promoting equity, and increasing cultural aptitude is an active process that requires continuous education and awareness in all aspects of our organization. new_jobs.title: Director of Advocacy
new_jobs.description:
**SUMMARY**
Reporting to the President/CEO, the Director of Advocacy (DA) is responsible for leading the affiliate’s advocacy efforts by promoting policies and systems that eliminate barriers to affordable housing. The Director of Advocacy will lead the department in conducting public education and awareness activities to highlight the importance of housing affordability in our community and build political will for change.
This is a full\-time, exempt position requiring flexibility to work evening and weekend hours as needed for meetings and projects.
**ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS**
**Policy Advocacy and Governmental Affairs**
* Provide leadership in setting and implementing Habitat Greater Raleigh’s advocacy agenda, policy priorities, and strategy.
* Lead Habitat Greater Raleigh’s policy advocacy efforts, which are primarily focused at the local/regional level with some engagement at state and national levels in collaboration with Habitat North Carolina and Habitat for Humanity International (HFHI).
* Champion Habitat Greater Raleigh’s participation in HFHI’s national advocacy campaigns.
* Build and maintain strong relationships with local, state, and national policymakers to position Habitat Greater Raleigh as a trusted advisor on housing issues.
* Support the development review processes for Habitat Greater Raleigh projects and mobilize the network to contact elected officials in support when needed.

**Public Awareness and Education**
* Speak on behalf of Habitat Greater Raleigh to promote its advocacy agenda in collaboration with the CEO.
* Raise awareness for equitable housing policies and call out systems that perpetuate the need for Habitat, especially through a racial justice lens.

Partner with the Communications department to set advocacy communications strategy, including key messaging, media, public relations and online engagement.
* Position Habitat Greater Raleigh as a thought leader and resource on affordable housing, especially regarding homeownership.
* Educate policymakers and community leaders on housing policy and issues affecting Habitat Greater Raleigh’s ability to further its mission.

**Community Partnerships**
* Forge partnerships with organizations across the housing continuum so that all may work together to advance access to housing affordability.
* Facilitate Habitat Greater Raleigh’s involvement in network\-wide advocacy events, including Habitat NC Legislative Day, Habitat NC Week of Action, Habitat on the Hill, etc.

**Grassroots Mobilizing**
* Lead efforts to engage Habitat Greater Raleigh’s constituency with housing advocacy messages and present opportunities for stakeholders to take action.
* Recruit board members, advocacy committee and homebuyers to participate in HFHI’s national lobby day, Habitat on the Hill.
* Work with the Homebuyer Support team to empower homebuyers and neighborhood residents to become housing advocates.
* Partner with the Faith Relations department to engage faith coalitions in advocacy from a faith\-based justice perspective.


**Strategic Planning and Departmental Leadership**
* Set the strategy for Habitat Greater Raleigh’s advocacy initiatives in collaboration with the Advocacy Committee and CEO.
* Represent the advocacy function on Habitat Greater Raleigh’s senior leadership team.
* Supervise the Advocacy Specialist role and other staff/intern/volunteer positions within the department.
* Manage department budget and track expenditures.
* Report advocacy metrics and progress on a monthly basis.
* Develop monthly reports for the Advocacy Committee and Board of Directors.
* Engage in strategic planning to set annual objectives within the context of Habitat Greater Raleigh’s organizational goals.


**Advocacy Committee**
* Lead the Advocacy Committee in shaping Habitat Greater Raleigh’s advocacy agenda and setting annual policy priorities that guide the affiliates advocacy work.
* Recruit for the Advocacy Committee and onboard new members.


**Knowledge, Skills and Abilities Required**
* Knowledge of affordable housing issues and policy at the local, state and/or national level
* Experience developing and managing efforts to influence policymakers and external stakeholders.
* Ability to think strategically and use judgement in decision\-making.
* Prioritize work to meet objectives when balancing multiple projects with differing deadlines, importance, and stakeholders.
* Strong relational, analytical, writing and verbal skills for communicating with diverse populations.
* Ability to establish and maintain collaborative working relationships with Habitat Greater Raleigh staff, Advocacy Committee, community partners, and policymakers.
* Ability to organize work and time while working efficiently and effectively in an office with frequent interruptions.
* Self\-motivated with the ability to work independently and as part of a team and with great energy and persistence.
* Excellent public relations skills with a passion for the organization’s mission.
* Critical thinking, problem solving, accuracy and attention to detail.
* Experience effectively managing a team.


**MINIMUM EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS**
* 5\+ years of experience in advocacy, government relations, public affairs and/or awareness campaigns aimed at influencing policy change.
* Excellent computer skills including Microsoft Office Suite, Office 365, and virtual meeting platforms.
* Familiarity with data sets such as U.S. Census, Comprehensive Housing Affordability Strategy (CHAS), HUD Income Limits, HUD Fair Market Rents (FMR) preferred.
* Willingness to pursue ongoing training (e.g., affordable housing \& advocacy) and professional development.
* Bachelor’s degree in political science, public affairs, public policy, communications, or related field preferred, but not required
